   241→## How It Works
   242→
   243→### Git Hook Workflow
   244→
   245→```
   246→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   247→│  Developer runs: git commit                                  │
   248→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   249→                     │
   250→                     ▼
   251→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   252→│  Pre-commit hook executes (.git/hooks/pre-commit)           │
   253→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   254→                     │
   255→                     ▼
   256→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   257→│  Check: Are any src/ files staged?                          │
   258→│  (src/, components/, widgets/, stores/, services/)          │
   259→└────────────┬────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┘
   260→             │ YES                    │ NO
   261→             ▼                        ▼
   262→┌──────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
   263→│ Check: Is tasks/*.md     │  │ Allow commit                 │
   264→│ file staged?             │  │ (doc-only, config, etc.)     │
   265→└──────┬───────────┬───────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
   266→       │ YES       │ NO
   267→       ▼           ▼
   268→┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   269→│ ✅ Allow   │  │ ❌ BLOCK commit                             │
   270→│ commit     │  │ Display error message with instructions     │
   271→└────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   272→```
   273→
   274→### Task File Lifecycle
   275→
   276→```
   277→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   278→│  1. Start Task                                               │
   279→│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
   280→│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
   281→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   282→                     │
   283→                     ▼
   284→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   285→│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
   286→│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
   287→│     - Create todo checklist with time estimates             │
   288→│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
   289→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   290→                     │
   291→                     ▼
   292→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   293→│  3. Implementation                                           │
   294→│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
   295→│     - Add implementation notes                               │
   296→│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
   297→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   298→                     │
   299→                     ▼
   300→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   301→│  4. Completion                                               │
   302→│     - Fill out review section                                │
   303→│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
   304→│     - Add testing status, time comparison                    │
   305→│     - Update docs/ if architecture changed                   │
   306→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   307→                     │
   308→                     ▼
   309→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   310→│  5. Commit                                                   │
   311→│     - Stage code files: git add src/                        │
   312→│     - Stage task file: git add tasks/[feature].md          │
   313→│     - Pre-commit hook verifies both staged                   │
   314→│     - Commit succeeds ✅                                     │
   315→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   316→```
   317→
   318→---
   319→
   320→## Troubleshooting
   321→
   322→### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running
   323→
   324→**Symptoms:**
   325→- Can commit code without task file
   326→- No error message appears
   327→
   328→**Causes & Fixes:**
   329→
   330→**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
   331→```bash
   332→# Fix: Make it executable
   333→chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
   334→```
   335→
   336→**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
   337→```bash
   338→# Check if file exists
   339→ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   340→
   341→# If missing, recreate from template (if you have one)
   342→# Or re-run setup script
   343→```
   344→
   345→**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
   346→```bash
   347→# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
   348→git commit  # ✅ Correct
   349→git commit --no-verify  # ❌ Bypasses hook
   350→```
   351→
   352→**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
   353→```bash
   354→# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe or PowerShell)
   355→# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
   356→# Note: On Windows, you may need to use Git Bash
   357→```
   358→
   359→---
   360→
   361→### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits
   362→
   363→**Symptoms:**
   364→- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
   365→- False positive error
   366→
   367→**Causes & Fixes:**
   368→
   369→**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
   370→```bash
   371→# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
   372→git add tasks/my-feature.md  # ✅ Correct
   373→git add my-feature.md         # ❌ Wrong location
   374→git add docs/task.md          # ❌ Wrong location
   375→```
   376→
   377→**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
   378→```bash
   379→# Check what's staged
   380→git diff --cached --name-only
   381→
   382→# Ensure task file is in the list
   383→# If not, stage it:
   384→git add tasks/my-feature.md
   385→```
   386→
   387→**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
   388→```bash
   389→# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
   390→git add tasks/my-feature.md   # ✅ Correct
   391→git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # ❌ Wrong extension
   392→```
   393→
   394→---
   395→
   396→### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template
   397→
   398→**Symptoms:**
   399→- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
   400→- File not found error
   401→
   402→**Fixes:**
   403→```bash
   404→# Check if template exists
   405→ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md
   406→
   407→# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
   408→ls -la .claude/
   409→
   410→# Ensure you're in repository root
   411→pwd  # Should end with tjn.portfolio
   412→```
   413→
   414→---
   415→
   416→### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File
   417→
   418→**Symptoms:**
   419→- Code commits go through without task file
   420→- Hook seems to not detect code changes
   421→
   422→**Causes & Fixes:**
   423→
   424→**Cause 1:** Code not in src/ directory
   425→```bash
   426→# Hook only checks src/ directory
   427→# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it
   428→
   429→# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
   430→# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
   431→# Find line: CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/')
   432→# Add more patterns: grep -E '^(src/|lib/|components/)'
   433→```
   434→
   435→**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
   436→```bash
   437→# Hook ignores .md files in src/
   438→# (to allow README updates without task file)
   439→
   440→# This is intentional behavior
   441→```
   442→
   443→---
   444→
   445→### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying
   446→
   447→**Symptoms:**
   448→- Commit blocked but no error message shown
   449→- Silent failure
   450→
   451→**Fixes:**
   452→```bash
   453→# Test hook manually
   454→bash .git/hooks/pre-commit
   455→
   456→# Check for syntax errors
   457→bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit
   458→
   459→# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
   460→# Some terminals don't support color codes
   461→```
   462→
   463→---
   464→
   465→## Customization
   466→
   467→### Adding More File Patterns to Hook
   468→
   469→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)
   470→
   471→```bash
   472→# Current pattern (only src/)
   473→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')
   474→
   475→# Add more directories (lib/, config/, etc.)
   476→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
   477→```
   478→
   479→### Excluding Specific Files from Hook
   480→
   481→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)
   482→
   483→```bash
   484→# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
   485→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$)')
   486→```
   487→
   488→### Customizing Task Template
   489→
   490→**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   491→
   492→Add/remove sections as needed:
   493→- Additional checklists
   494→- Project-specific requirements
   495→- Custom metadata fields
   496→
   497→**Example additions:**
   498→```markdown
   499→## Security Checklist
   500→- [ ] Input validation added
   501→- [ ] XSS prevention verified
   502→- [ ] SQL injection prevented
   503→
   504→## Performance Checklist
   505→- [ ] Bundle size impact < 10KB
   506→- [ ] No memory leaks
   507→- [ ] Animations 60fps
   508→```
   509→
   510→### Adding New Custom Commands
   511→
   512→**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`
   513→
   514→Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`
   515→
   516→```markdown
   517→# Review Task Checklist
   518→
   519→Before marking task complete:
   520→
   521→- [ ] Review section filled out
   522→- [ ] All files documented
   523→- [ ] Testing complete
   524→- [ ] Documentation updated
   525→- [ ] Bundle size checked
   526→```
   527→
   528→### Adjusting Hook Error Messages
   529→
   530→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Lines 30-70)
   531→
   532→Customize:
   533→- Error message text
   534→- Color scheme (ANSI codes)
   535→- Instructions provided
   536→- Examples shown
   537→
   538→---
   539→
   540→## Best Practices
   541→
   542→### 1. Always Start with the Checklist
   543→
   544→Before starting any task:
   545→```bash
   546→# Read the workflow checklist
   547→cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   548→
   549→# Or use custom command
   550→/start-task  # If available in Claude Code
   551→```
   552→
   553→### 2. Create Task File FIRST
   554→
   555→```bash
   556→# ✅ CORRECT ORDER
   557→cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
   558→# Fill out the task file
   559→# THEN start coding
   560→
   561→# ❌ WRONG ORDER
   562→# Start coding first
   563→# Create task file later (will forget details!)
   564→```
   565→
   566→### 3. Update Task File as You Work
   567→
   568→```markdown
   569→## Todo Checklist
   570→
   571→### Phase 1
   572→- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE (took 30 min)
   573→- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
   574→- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for user input)
   575→```
   576→
   577→Keep the task file up to date, don't batch updates at the end.
   578→
   579→### 4. Fill Out Review Section Immediately
   580→
   581→When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
   582→- Summary of changes
   583→- Files created/modified
   584→- Testing status
   585→- Issues encountered
   586→
   587→Don't wait days/weeks - you'll forget important details!
   588→
   589→### 5. Stage Task File with Code
   590→
   591→```bash
   592→# ✅ CORRECT - Stage both together
   593→git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   594→git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
   595→git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"
   596→
   597→# ❌ WRONG - Separate commits
   598→git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   599→git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
   600→# Oops, forgot task file! (Hook will block this)
   601→```
   602→
   603→### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names
   604→
   605→```bash
   606→# ✅ GOOD - Descriptive, specific
   607→tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
   608→tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
   609→tasks/refactor-zustand-stores.md
   610→
   611→# ❌ BAD - Vague, generic
   612→tasks/todo.md
   613→tasks/task1.md
   614→tasks/stuff.md
   615→tasks/changes.md
   616→```
   617→
   618→### 7. One Task File Per Feature
   619→
   620→Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.
   621→
   622→```bash
   623→# ✅ CORRECT - Separate files
   624→tasks/add-search-feature.md
   625→tasks/add-export-feature.md
   626→
   627→# ❌ WRONG - One giant file
   628→tasks/all-features.md
   629→```
   630→
   631→### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES
   632→
   633→```bash
   634→# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
   635→git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
   636→git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
   637→git commit --no-verify  # Doc-only changes (maybe)
   638→
   639→# NOT for:
   640→git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
   641→git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later" (you won't!)
   642→```
   643→
   644→### 9. Keep Documentation Updated
   645→
   646→If architecture changes, update docs:
   647→```bash
   648→git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
   649→git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
   650→git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md  # New architecture doc
   651→git commit -m "feat: Add undo system
   652→
   653→Closes #42"
   654→```
   655→
   656→### 10. Reference CLAUDE.md Rules
   657→
   658→When in doubt, check CLAUDE.md:
   659→```bash
   660→# Quick reference
   661→cat CLAUDE.md | grep "^[0-9])"
   662→
   663→# Full read
   664→cat CLAUDE.md
   665→```
   666→
   667→---
   668→
   669→## Integration with Claude Code
   670→
   671→### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code
   672→
   673→1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
   674→2. **Use task template** for all planning
   675→3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
   676→4. **Fill review section** when done
   677→5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged
   678→
   679→### Behavioral Changes for Claude Code
   680→
   681→**Before starting ANY task:**
   682→- ✅ Read `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   683→- ✅ Create task file from template
   684→- ✅ Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)
   685→
   686→**During implementation:**
   687→- ✅ Update todos as you complete them
   688→- ✅ Document decisions in task file
   689→- ✅ Track time estimates vs actual
   690→
   691→**When complete:**
   692→- ✅ Fill out review section
   693→- ✅ Update docs/ if architecture changed
   694→- ✅ Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)
   695→
   696→**On every commit:**
   697→- ✅ Stage code files AND task file together
   698→- ✅ Git hook verifies compliance
   699→- ✅ Commit proceeds
   700→
   701→---
   702→
   703→## Appendix
   704→
   705→### File Structure Reference
   706→
   707→```
   708→tjn.portfolio/
   709→├── .git/
   710→│   └── hooks/
   711→│       └── pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
   712→├── .claude/
   713→│   ├── automation-guide.md         # This file
   714→│   ├── workflow-checklist.md       # Step-by-step checklist
   715→│   ├── templates/
   716→│   │   └── task-template.md        # Task file template
   717→│   └── skills/                     # Claude skills
   718→├── tasks/
   719→│   └── [feature-name].md           # Task files (one per feature)
   720→├── CLAUDE.md                        # Main workflow guide
   721→├── README.md                        # Comprehensive project documentation
   722→├── index.html                       # V1 main entry point
   723→└── new/                             # V2 React development
   724→```
   725→
   726→### Git Hook Exit Codes
   727→
   728→- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
   729→- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)
   730→
   731→### ANSI Color Codes Used
   732→
   733→```bash
   734→RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
   735→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
   736→GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
   737→BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
   738→NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
   739→```
   740→
   741→---
   742→
   743→## Support & Questions
   744→
   745→**For questions about:**
   746→- **CLAUDE.md workflow:** See `CLAUDE.md` in root
   747→- **Workflow steps:** See `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   748→- **Task templates:** See `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   749→- **Git hook issues:** See troubleshooting section above
   750→- **Feature requests:** Create GitHub issue
   751→
   752→---
   753→
   754→**Last Updated:** December 9, 2025
   755→**Project:** Travis J. Neuman Professional IT Portfolio
   756→**Version:** 1.1
   757→
   758→**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. They catch mistakes BEFORE they become problems. Follow the workflow, and your development process will be smoother and more consistent.
   759→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide - TJN Portfolio

**Version:** 1.1
**Last Updated:** December 9, 2025
**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools
**Project:** Travis J. Neuman Professional IT Portfolio

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview](#overview)
2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
7. [Customization](#customization)
8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)

---

## Overview

This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md workflow by:

✅ **Preventing commits** without task file updates
✅ **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation
✅ **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks
✅ **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists

**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.

---

## Quick Start

### For Developers

1. **Before starting any task:**
   ```bash
   # Check workflow checklist
   cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   ```

2. **Create task file from template:**
   ```bash
   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   ```

3. **Git hook automatically enforces:**
   - Task file must be staged when committing code changes
   - Will block commit if missing

### For Claude Code

1. **Use `/start-task` command** (if available)
2. **Reference workflow checklist** before starting
3. **Use task template** for all new tasks
4. **Git hook will catch** if task file missing

---

## Automation Tools

### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook

**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Workflow Process

**How it works:**
1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)

**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  ❌ COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.

Changed code files:
  - src/components/Modal.tsx
  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts

Required Action:
  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
  3. Retry commit: git commit
```

**Emergency Bypass:**
```bash
git commit --no-verify
```
⚠️ **Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies

---

### 2. Workflow Checklist

**Location:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
**Enforces:** Complete CLAUDE.md workflow

**When to use:**
- Before starting ANY task
- During implementation (reference as needed)
- Before marking work complete
- Before creating git commits

**Sections:**
- ✅ Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
- ✅ During Implementation
- ✅ When Complete
- ✅ Git Commit Checklist
- ✅ Rules #1-11 Quick Reference

**View checklist:**
```bash
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
```

---

### 3. Task Template

**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Workflow Process

**Sections included:**
- Overview and problem statement
- Files to read
- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
- Todo checklist with time estimates
- Design considerations
- Implementation notes
- Testing checklist
- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)

**Create new task:**
```bash
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Good naming examples:**
- ✅ `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
- ✅ `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
- ✅ `tasks/notes-ux-improvements.md`

**Bad naming examples:**
- ❌ `tasks/todo.md`
- ❌ `tasks/task1.md`
- ❌ `tasks/new.md`

---

### 4. Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow reminders

#### `/start-task` Command

**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
**Purpose:** Comprehensive reminder before starting any task

**What it covers:**
1. Sync with main branch (Rule #11)
2. Read CLAUDE.md (Rules #1-11)
3. Create task file from template
4. Fill out task plan
5. Get user approval (Rule #3)
6. Implementation workflow
7. Review section and documentation updates

**Use when:**
- Starting a new task
- Unsure of the workflow
- Need a quick reference

---

## Setup Instructions

### Initial Setup (One-Time)

The automation tools are already set up in this repository. No additional installation required.

**Verify setup:**
```bash
# Check git hook exists and is executable
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check automation files exist
ls -la .claude/

# Expected output:
# .claude/
# ├── automation-guide.md         (this file)
# ├── workflow-checklist.md       (step-by-step checklist)
# ├── templates/
# │   └── task-template.md        (task file template)
# └── commands/
#     └── start-task.md           (custom command)
```

### For New Developers

1. **Clone repository**
2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (especially rules #1-11)
3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
5. **Test git hook:**
   ```bash
   # Make a small change to src/ file
   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx

   # Try to commit without staging task file
   git add src/main.tsx
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should be BLOCKED

   # Stage task file and retry
   git add tasks/some-task.md
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should succeed

   # Revert test
   git reset HEAD~1
   git checkout src/main.tsx
   ```

---

## How It Works

### Git Hook Workflow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Developer runs: git commit                                  │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Pre-commit hook executes (.git/hooks/pre-commit)           │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Check: Are any src/ files staged?                          │
│  (src/, components/, widgets/, stores/, services/)          │
└────────────┬────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┘
             │ YES                    │ NO
             ▼                        ▼
┌──────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ Check: Is tasks/*.md     │  │ Allow commit                 │
│ file staged?             │  │ (doc-only, config, etc.)     │
└──────┬───────────┬───────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
       │ YES       │ NO
       ▼           ▼
┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ✅ Allow   │  │ ❌ BLOCK commit                             │
│ commit     │  │ Display error message with instructions     │
└────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Task File Lifecycle

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. Start Task                                               │
│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
│     - Create todo checklist with time estimates             │
│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  3. Implementation                                           │
│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
│     - Add implementation notes                               │
│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  4. Completion                                               │
│     - Fill out review section                                │
│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
│     - Add testing status, time comparison                    │
│     - Update docs/ if architecture changed                   │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  5. Commit                                                   │
│     - Stage code files: git add src/                        │
│     - Stage task file: git add tasks/[feature].md          │
│     - Pre-commit hook verifies both staged                   │
│     - Commit succeeds ✅                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Troubleshooting

### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running

**Symptoms:**
- Can commit code without task file
- No error message appears

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
```bash
# Fix: Make it executable
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
```

**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
```bash
# Check if file exists
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# If missing, recreate from template (if you have one)
# Or re-run setup script
```

**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
```bash
# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
git commit  # ✅ Correct
git commit --no-verify  # ❌ Bypasses hook
```

**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
```bash
# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe or PowerShell)
# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
# Note: On Windows, you may need to use Git Bash
```

---

### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits

**Symptoms:**
- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
- False positive error

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
```bash
# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
git add tasks/my-feature.md  # ✅ Correct
git add my-feature.md         # ❌ Wrong location
git add docs/task.md          # ❌ Wrong location
```

**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
```bash
# Check what's staged
git diff --cached --name-only

# Ensure task file is in the list
# If not, stage it:
git add tasks/my-feature.md
```

**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
```bash
# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
git add tasks/my-feature.md   # ✅ Correct
git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # ❌ Wrong extension
```

---

### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template

**Symptoms:**
- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
- File not found error

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Check if template exists
ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md

# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
ls -la .claude/

# Ensure you're in repository root
pwd  # Should end with tjn.portfolio
```

---

### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File

**Symptoms:**
- Code commits go through without task file
- Hook seems to not detect code changes

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Code not in src/ directory
```bash
# Hook only checks src/ directory
# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it

# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
# Find line: CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/')
# Add more patterns: grep -E '^(src/|lib/|components/)'
```

**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
```bash
# Hook ignores .md files in src/
# (to allow README updates without task file)

# This is intentional behavior
```

---

### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying

**Symptoms:**
- Commit blocked but no error message shown
- Silent failure

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Test hook manually
bash .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check for syntax errors
bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
# Some terminals don't support color codes
```

---

## Customization

### Adding More File Patterns to Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)

```bash
# Current pattern (only src/)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')

# Add more directories (lib/, config/, etc.)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
```

### Excluding Specific Files from Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)

```bash
# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$)')
```

### Customizing Task Template

**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`

Add/remove sections as needed:
- Additional checklists
- Project-specific requirements
- Custom metadata fields

**Example additions:**
```markdown
## Security Checklist
- [ ] Input validation added
- [ ] XSS prevention verified
- [ ] SQL injection prevented

## Performance Checklist
- [ ] Bundle size impact < 10KB
- [ ] No memory leaks
- [ ] Animations 60fps
```

### Adding New Custom Commands

**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`

Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`

```markdown
# Review Task Checklist

Before marking task complete:

- [ ] Review section filled out
- [ ] All files documented
- [ ] Testing complete
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Bundle size checked
```

### Adjusting Hook Error Messages

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Lines 30-70)

Customize:
- Error message text
- Color scheme (ANSI codes)
- Instructions provided
- Examples shown

---

## Best Practices

### 1. Always Start with the Checklist

Before starting any task:
```bash
# Read the workflow checklist
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md

# Or use custom command
/start-task  # If available in Claude Code
```

### 2. Create Task File FIRST

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT ORDER
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
# Fill out the task file
# THEN start coding

# ❌ WRONG ORDER
# Start coding first
# Create task file later (will forget details!)
```

### 3. Update Task File as You Work

```markdown
## Todo Checklist

### Phase 1
- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE (took 30 min)
- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for user input)
```

Keep the task file up to date, don't batch updates at the end.

### 4. Fill Out Review Section Immediately

When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
- Summary of changes
- Files created/modified
- Testing status
- Issues encountered

Don't wait days/weeks - you'll forget important details!

### 5. Stage Task File with Code

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT - Stage both together
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"

# ❌ WRONG - Separate commits
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
# Oops, forgot task file! (Hook will block this)
```

### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names

```bash
# ✅ GOOD - Descriptive, specific
tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
tasks/refactor-zustand-stores.md

# ❌ BAD - Vague, generic
tasks/todo.md
tasks/task1.md
tasks/stuff.md
tasks/changes.md
```

### 7. One Task File Per Feature

Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT - Separate files
tasks/add-search-feature.md
tasks/add-export-feature.md

# ❌ WRONG - One giant file
tasks/all-features.md
```

### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES

```bash
# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
git commit --no-verify  # Doc-only changes (maybe)

# NOT for:
git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later" (you won't!)
```

### 9. Keep Documentation Updated

If architecture changes, update docs:
```bash
git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md  # New architecture doc
git commit -m "feat: Add undo system

Closes #42"
```

### 10. Reference CLAUDE.md Rules

When in doubt, check CLAUDE.md:
```bash
# Quick reference
cat CLAUDE.md | grep "^[0-9])"

# Full read
cat CLAUDE.md
```

---

## Integration with Claude Code

### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code

1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
2. **Use task template** for all planning
3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
4. **Fill review section** when done
5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged

### Behavioral Changes for Claude Code

**Before starting ANY task:**
- ✅ Read `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
- ✅ Create task file from template
- ✅ Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)

**During implementation:**
- ✅ Update todos as you complete them
- ✅ Document decisions in task file
- ✅ Track time estimates vs actual

**When complete:**
- ✅ Fill out review section
- ✅ Update docs/ if architecture changed
- ✅ Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)

**On every commit:**
- ✅ Stage code files AND task file together
- ✅ Git hook verifies compliance
- ✅ Commit proceeds

---

## Appendix

### File Structure Reference

```
tjn.portfolio/
├── .git/
│   └── hooks/
│       └── pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
├── .claude/
│   ├── automation-guide.md         # This file
│   ├── workflow-checklist.md       # Step-by-step checklist
│   ├── templates/
│   │   └── task-template.md        # Task file template
│   └── skills/                     # Claude skills
├── tasks/
│   └── [feature-name].md           # Task files (one per feature)
├── CLAUDE.md                        # Main workflow guide
├── README.md                        # Comprehensive project documentation
├── index.html                       # V1 main entry point
└── new/                             # V2 React development
```

### Git Hook Exit Codes

- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)

### ANSI Color Codes Used

```bash
RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
```

---

## Support & Questions

**For questions about:**
- **CLAUDE.md workflow:** See `CLAUDE.md` in root
- **Workflow steps:** See `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
- **Task templates:** See `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
- **Git hook issues:** See troubleshooting section above
- **Feature requests:** Create GitHub issue

---

**Last Updated:** December 9, 2025
**Project:** Travis J. Neuman Professional IT Portfolio
**Version:** 1.1

**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. They catch mistakes BEFORE they become problems. Follow the workflow, and your development process will be smoother and more consistent.

# CLAUDE.md Compliance Checklist

**Purpose:** Ensure every development task follows the mandatory CLAUDE.md workflow
**Usage:** Reference this checklist BEFORE starting ANY coding task
**Project:** tdr.portfolio (thedeadrobot.com)

---

## Pre-Flight Checklist

### Before Starting ANY Task

- [ ] **Read CLAUDE.md** (especially rules #1-15)
- [ ] **Sync with master branch:** `git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit`
- [ ] **Create task file:** `tasks/[feature-name].md` using template from `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
- [ ] **Fill out task plan:**
  - Problem statement
  - Files to read
  - Proposed changes
  - Todo checklist
- [ ] **Get user approval** via `ExitPlanMode` tool (Rule #3)
- [ ] **ONLY THEN** begin implementation

---

## During Implementation

### As You Work

- [ ] **Update todos** as you complete each item (Rule #4)
- [ ] **Make changes as simple as possible** (Rule #9)
  - Impact minimal code
  - Avoid complex refactors unless necessary
  - Keep changes focused on the task
- [ ] **Give high-level explanations** of changes (Rule #5)
- [ ] **No temporary fixes** - Find and fix root causes (Rule #8)

---

## Landing Page Changes

### Before ANY UI Change

- [ ] **Screenshot current state**
- [ ] **Make ONE change** (not multiple at once)
- [ ] **Test in browsers:** Chrome, Firefox, Safari
- [ ] **Test responsiveness:** 375px, 768px, 1024px
- [ ] **Compare to baseline** (visual diff)
- [ ] **Check animations work**
- [ ] **Get user approval** before committing

### Sacred Elements (NEVER CHANGE)

- [ ] `<div>` containers (NOT `<button>`, `<main>`, `<nav>`)
- [ ] `#title-container` with `#title` and `#arrow`
- [ ] `#social-menu` dropdown with 3 links
- [ ] `#gif-container` with animated GIF
- [ ] Title pulse animation (white ↔ green)
- [ ] Dropdown animations (stagger, transition)
- [ ] NO focus borders/outlines
- [ ] NO letter hover effects

---

## Automation Changes

### Before ANY Automation Change

- [ ] **Test with DRY_RUN=true:** `DRY_RUN=true python automation/generate_post.py`
- [ ] **Verify output matches brand voice**
- [ ] **Check validation rules work**
- [ ] **Test edge cases**
- [ ] **Update docs** if behavior changes

---

## When Complete

### Before Marking Work "Done"

- [ ] **Add review section** to `tasks/[feature].md` (Rule #7)
  - Summary of changes
  - Files created/modified
  - Testing status
  - Issues encountered
- [ ] **Update documentation** if architecture changed (Rule #10)
- [ ] **Visual inspection passed** (all browsers)
- [ ] **Animations working** (60fps)
- [ ] **Page weight acceptable** (< 50KB excluding GIF)

---

## Git Commit Checklist

### Before Creating Commit

- [ ] **Review changes:** `git diff`
- [ ] **Write clear commit message:**
  ```
  type: Short description (50 chars max)

  - Bullet point details
  - What changed and why
  - Related issues

  🤖 Generated with Claude Code
  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
  ```
- [ ] **Commit types:** feat, fix, docs, style, chore
- [ ] **Push to master:** `git push origin master`

---

## Rules #1-15 Quick Reference

### CLAUDE.md Core Rules (IMMUTABLE)

1. **Think → Plan → Write to tasks/todo.md** - Create task file FIRST
2. **Plan has todo list** - Checklist with items to check off
3. **Check with me before working** - Get approval via ExitPlanMode
4. **Begin working, mark tasks complete** - Update todos as you go
5. **High-level explanations only** - Don't overexplain every detail
6. **Make changes as simple as possible** - Minimal code impact
7. **Add review section** when done - Document what changed
8. **DO NOT BE LAZY** - Find root cause, no temporary fixes
9. **Simplicity is key** - Impact minimal code, avoid bugs
10. **Update documentation** - Keep docs detailed, clear, clean
11. **Sync with master** at start of EVERY session - Stay up to date
12. **Perfect foundation** - Build for 100% even though it's 1% complete
13. **Complete logical units** - Never stop mid-implementation
14. **Validate current date** - Check `<env>` section for today's date
15. **Commit & Push at end** - Git add, commit with details, push

---

## Emergency Bypass

### When to Override

Skip workflow ONLY for:
- Hotfix deployments (site down)
- Reverting broken commits
- Critical security issues

**Otherwise:** Always follow the full checklist.

---

## Questions?

- See: `CLAUDE.md` for full workflow guide
- See: `.claude/automation-guide.md` for automation help
- See: `.claude/skills/README.md` for available skills
- See: `tasks/` directory for example task files

---

**Remember:** These rules exist to ensure:
- Quality code
- Complete documentation
- No forgotten tasks
- Consistent workflow

**Following this checklist = Better code + Happier users** ✅

5. **High-level explanations only** - Don't overexplain every detail
6. **Make changes as simple as possible** - Minimal code impact
7. **Add review section** when done - Document what changed
8. **DO NOT BE LAZY** - Find root cause, no temporary fixes
9. **Simplicity is key** - Impact minimal code, avoid bugs
10. **Update documentation** - Keep docs detailed, clear, clean
11. **Sync with main** at start of EVERY session - Stay up to date

---

## Automation Tools

### Available Helpers

- **Git Pre-Commit Hook:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
  - Blocks commits when code changes without task file updates
  - Ensures compliance with Rule #1-2
  - Can bypass with `--no-verify` (emergencies only)

- **Task Template:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
  - Standard format for all task files
  - Includes review section template
  - Maps to all CLAUDE.md rules

- **Custom Commands:** `.claude/commands/`
  - `/start-task` - Reminder to follow workflow
  - Add more as needed

- **Automation Guide:** `.claude/automation-guide.md`
  - Detailed setup instructions
  - Troubleshooting tips
  - Customization guide

---

## Emergency Bypass

### When to Override

Use `git commit --no-verify` ONLY for:
- Hotfix deployments
- Documentation-only changes
- Reverting broken commits
- Emergencies (site down, critical bug)

**Otherwise:** Always follow the full checklist.

---

## Questions?

- See: `CLAUDE.md` for full workflow guide
- See: `.claude/automation-guide.md` for automation help
- See: `tasks/` directory for example task files

---

**Remember:** These rules exist to ensure:
- Quality code
- Complete documentation
- No forgotten tasks
- Consistent workflow
- Better collaboration

**Following this checklist = Better code + Happier users** ✅

content.content:
# Claude Code - Comprehensive Feature Overview

## Core Features

### What Claude Code Does
- **Build features from descriptions**: Convert plain English requirements into working code with planning and verification
- **Debug and fix issues**: Analyze codebases and implement fixes from error descriptions
- **Navigate codebases**: Answer questions about project structure with awareness of entire projects and web-accessible information
- **Automate tedious tasks**: Fix lint issues, resolve merge conflicts, write release notes

### Why Developers Choose Claude Code
- **Terminal-native**: Works within existing developer workflows without additional windows/IDEs
- **Takes action**: Directly edits files, runs commands, creates commits
- **Unix philosophy**: Composable and scriptable (e.g., `tail -f app.log | claude -p "alert if anomalies"`)
- **Enterprise-ready**: Security, privacy, and compliance built-in; supports AWS/GCP hosting

---

## Installation & Setup

### Quick Start (30 seconds)
```bash
# macOS/Linux (Homebrew)
brew install --cask claude-code

# macOS/Linux (curl)
curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# Windows (PowerShell)
irm https://claude.ai/install.ps1 | iex

# NPM (requires Node.js 18+)
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
```

Then authenticate and start:
```bash
cd your-project
claude
```

**Requirements**: 
- Claude.ai (recommended) or Claude Console account
- Auto-updates enabled by default

**Setup Documentation**: `/docs/en/setup` for advanced installation, manual updates, uninstall

---

## Configuration & Customization

### Settings Overview
- **Global & project-level settings**: Customize behavior per project
- **Environment variables**: Configure via CLI flags and env vars
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/settings`

### Terminal Configuration
- Optimize terminal setup for best experience
- Shell integration guidelines
- **Reference**: `/docs/en/terminal-config`

### Model Configuration
- Model aliases like `opusplan`
- **Reference**: `/docs/en/model-config`

### Memory Management
- Manage Claude's memory across sessions
- Multiple memory location options
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/memory`

### Status Line Configuration
- Create custom status lines displaying contextual information
- **Reference**: `/docs/en/statusline`

---

## Interactive Features

### Slash Commands
Control Claude's behavior during interactive sessions:
- **Reference**: `/docs/en/slash-commands`

### Interactive Mode
- Keyboard shortcuts
- Input modes
- Session controls
- **Complete Reference**: `/docs/en/interactive-mode`

### Checkpointing
- Save and restore session states
- **Reference**: `/docs/en/checkpointing`

---

## Extension & Customization

### Hooks
Register shell commands to customize Claude Code behavior:
- **Getting Started**: `/docs/en/hooks-guide`
- **Complete Reference**: `/docs/en/hooks`

### Plugins System
Extend Claude Code with:
- Custom commands
- Agents
- Hooks
- Skills
- MCP servers
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/plugins`

### Agent Skills
- Create task-specific capabilities
- Share and manage Skills
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/skills`

### Subagents
- Create specialized AI subagents
- Task-specific workflows
- Improved context management
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/sub-agents`

### Output Styles
- Adapt Claude Code beyond software engineering
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/output-styles`

---

## Model Context Protocol (MCP)

Connect Claude Code to external tools and data sources:
- **Features**: Can read Google Drive docs, update Jira tickets, integrate custom developer tooling
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/mcp`
- **MCP Connectors**: https://claude.com/partners/mcp

---

## IDE Integration

### Visual Studio Code
- Native extension
- CLI integration
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/vs-code`

### JetBrains IDEs
- IntelliJ, PyCharm, WebStorm, and more
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/jetbrains`

---

## CI/CD Integration

### GitHub Actions
- Integrate Claude Code into development workflows
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/github-actions`

### GitLab CI/CD
- Deploy Claude Code in GitLab pipelines
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/gitlab-ci-cd`

### Slack Integration
- Delegate coding tasks from Slack
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/slack`

---

## Deployment Options

### Cloud Platforms
- **Amazon Bedrock**: `/docs/en/amazon-bedrock`
- **Google Vertex AI**: `/docs/en/google-vertex-ai`
- **Microsoft Foundry**: `/docs/en/microsoft-foundry`

### Enterprise Configuration
- **Network Config**: Proxies, custom CAs, mTLS
- **LLM Gateway**: Integration with gateway solutions
- **Development Containers**: Consistent environments for teams
- **Sandboxing**: Filesystem and network isolation

### Desktop App
- Local or cloud infrastructure execution
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/desktop`

### Web Version
- Asynchronous task execution
- Secure cloud infrastructure
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web`

### Headless Mode
- Programmatic execution without UI
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/headless`

---

## Security & Administration

### Security
- Built-in safeguards and best practices
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/security`

### Data Usage
- Privacy policies and data handling
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/data-usage`

### Identity & Access Management (IAM)
- User authentication and authorization
- Access controls for organizations
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/iam`

### Monitoring & Analytics
- OpenTelemetry configuration
- Usage insights and productivity metrics
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/monitoring-usage` & `/docs/en/analytics`

### Cost Management
- Track and optimize token usage
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/costs`

### Plugin Marketplaces
- Distribute extensions across teams
- **Documentation**: `/docs/en/plugin-marketplaces`

---

## Common Workflows & Troubleshooting

- **Common Workflows**: `/docs/en/common-workflows` (includes git worktrees, parallel sessions)
- **Troubleshooting**: `/docs/en/troubleshooting`

---

## Additional Resources

- **Claude Developer Platform**: https://platform.claude.com/
- **Claude Code on Web**: https://claude.ai/code
- **Agent SDK**: https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/agent-sdk/overview
- **Reference Implementation**: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/tree/main/.devcontainer
- **Courses**: https://www.anthropic.com/learn

file.content:
---
description: Execute prompt(s) from ./prompts/ with automatic archiving to .archive/completed-prompts/
arguments:
  - name: prompt_ids
    description: "Prompt number(s) or name(s) to run. Examples: '001', '5 6 7', 'user-auth'. Use --parallel or --sequential for multiple prompts."
    required: false
---

# Run Prompt with Auto-Archive

Execute one or more prompts from `./prompts/` directory, then automatically archive completed prompts to `.archive/completed-prompts/` per CLAUDE.md session protocol.

## Workflow

1. Delegate to `/taches-cc-resources:run-prompt` to execute the prompt(s)
2. After successful completion, move prompt(s) from `./prompts/completed/` to `.archive/completed-prompts/`
3. Clean up any temporary directories

## Implementation

**Step 1:** Execute the tache run-prompt command with all arguments passed through

```
/taches-cc-resources:run-prompt {{prompt_ids}}
```

**Step 2:** After completion, check if `./prompts/completed/` exists and move contents to archive

```bash
# If prompts/completed/ exists, move all files to archive and cleanup
if [ -d "./prompts/completed" ]; then
  mkdir -p ".archive/completed-prompts"
  mv ./prompts/completed/* .archive/completed-prompts/ 2>/dev/null || true
  rmdir ./prompts/completed 2>/dev/null || true
fi
```

**Step 3:** Report final location of archived prompts

## Notes

- This wrapper ensures CLAUDE.md compliance (prompts archived to `.archive/completed-prompts/`)
- Transparent to user - just use `/run [prompt-id]` instead of `/run-prompt`
- Supports all `/run-prompt` features: single, parallel, sequential execution

1→---
     2→description: Intelligent task router - analyzes your request and automatically delegates to optimal workflow (plan/prompt/meta-prompt/direct execution)
     3→arguments:
     4→  - name: task_description
     5→    description: "Describe what you want to accomplish. Can be empty to check todos, or a full task description."
     6→    required: false
     7→---
     8→
     9→# Start Task - Intelligent Workflow Router
    10→
    11→Analyzes your task and automatically routes to the optimal execution strategy per CLAUDE.md's Intelligent Prompt Routing system.
    12→
    13→## Routing Logic
    14→
    15→**If no task description provided:**
    16→- Run `/check-todos` to list outstanding work
    17→- Await user selection
    18→
    19→**If task description provided, analyze and route:**
    20→
    21→### 1. Planning Tasks → `/create-plan`
    22→**Keywords:** "plan", "design", "architecture", "multi-phase", "roadmap"
    23→**Complexity:** Multi-phase features, complex architecture
    24→**Example:** "Plan the analytics dashboard feature"
    25→
    26→→ Invoke: `/create-plan {{task_description}}`
    27→
    28→### 2. Decision-Making → `/consider:*` frameworks
    29→**Keywords:** "should we", "which approach", "evaluate", "compare", "decide"
    30→**Examples:**
    31→- "Should we use Redux or Zustand?" → `/consider:first-principles`
    32→- "Which features to prioritize?" → `/consider:eisenhower-matrix`
    33→- "Is this the right architecture?" → `/consider:second-order`
    34→
    35→→ Analyze decision type and invoke appropriate framework
    36→
    37→### 3. Debugging Tasks → `debug-like-expert` skill
    38→**Keywords:** "debug", "intermittent", "mysterious", "sometimes fails", "race condition"
    39→**Example:** "Debug the intermittent calendar sync issue"
    40→
    41→→ Invoke: `Skill(debug-like-expert)` with task description
    42→
    43→### 4. Moderate Complexity → `/create-prompt` + `/run`
    44→**Criteria:**
    45→- 30min - 2hr estimated work
    46→- 3-10 files to modify
    47→- Clear scope but needs fresh context
    48→**Example:** "Refactor the widget loading system"
    49→
    50→→ Invoke: `/create-prompt {{task_description}}` then offer to `/run`
    51→
    52→### 5. Multi-Stage Features → `/create-meta-prompt`
    53→**Keywords:** "research then implement", "explore options then build", multi-stage
    54→**Example:** "Research authentication options then implement the best one"
    55→
    56→→ Invoke: `/create-meta-prompt {{task_description}}`
    57→
    58→### 6. Simple/Direct Tasks → Execute directly
    59→**Criteria:**
    60→- 1-2 files
    61→- <30min work
    62→- Clear, straightforward
    63→**Example:** "Fix the typo in the README"
    64→
    65→→ Execute directly in current context
    66→
    67→## Implementation
    68→
    69→**Step 1: Parse Arguments**
    70→```javascript
    71→const taskDescription = "{{task_description}}".trim();
    72→
    73→if (!taskDescription) {
    74→  // No task provided - check todos
    75→  return "/check-todos";
    76→}
    77→```
    78→
    79→**Step 2: Analyze Task**
    80→
    81→Examine task description for:
    82→- **Keywords** indicating task type
    83→- **Complexity signals** (file count, time estimate, scope)
    84→- **Execution pattern** (single-stage, multi-stage, research-heavy)
    85→
    86→**Step 3: Route to Optimal Workflow**
    87→
    88→Based on analysis:
    89→
    90→```javascript
    91→// Planning tasks
    92→if (matches("plan|design|architecture|multi-phase|roadmap")) {
    93→  invoke("/create-plan {{task_description}}");
    94→}
    95→
    96→// Decision-making tasks
    97→else if (matches("should we|which|evaluate|compare|decide")) {
    98→  determineFramework(); // first-principles, eisenhower-matrix, etc.
    99→  invoke("/consider:[framework] {{task_description}}");
   100→}
   101→
   102→// Debugging tasks
   103→else if (matches("debug|intermittent|mysterious|race condition")) {
   104→  invoke("Skill(debug-like-expert)");
   105→}
   106→
   107→// Moderate complexity - delegate to fresh context
   108→else if (estimatedComplexity === "moderate") {
   109→  invoke("/create-prompt {{task_description}}");
   110→  // Offer to run after creation
   111→}
   112→
   113→// Multi-stage features
   114→else if (matches("research then|explore then|multi-stage")) {
   115→  invoke("/create-meta-prompt {{task_description}}");
   116→}
   117→
   118→// Simple/direct - execute in current context
   119→else {
   120→  executeDirectly();
   121→}
   122→```
   123→
   124→**Step 4: Follow Through**
   125→
   126→After routing:
   127→- If created prompt/plan: offer to execute with `/run` or `/run-plan`
   128→- If direct execution: follow standard workflow (plan → approve → implement)
   129→- If framework: present analysis and next steps
   130→
   131→## Examples
   132→
   133→```bash
   134→# Automatically routes to /create-plan
   135→/start-task Plan the user authentication system
   136→
   137→# Automatically routes to /consider:first-principles
   138→/start-task Should we use WebSockets or Server-Sent Events?
   139→
   140→# Automatically routes to debug-like-expert
   141→/start-task Debug the intermittent widget loading failure
   142→
   143→# Automatically routes to /create-prompt
   144→/start-task Refactor the calendar view components
   145→
   146→# Automatically routes to /create-meta-prompt
   147→/start-task Research GraphQL options then implement the best approach
   148→
   149→# Direct execution
   150→/start-task Fix typo in README.md
   151→
   152→# Check todos (no argument)
   153→/start-task
   154→```
   155→
   156→## Notes
   157→
   158→- **Transparent routing:** User just describes task naturally
   159→- **Zero configuration:** Analysis and routing happen automatically
   160→- **Optimal execution:** Each task routed to best-fit workflow
   161→- **CLAUDE.md compliant:** Follows Intelligent Prompt Routing specification
   162→- **Composable:** Routes to other slash commands and skills as needed
   163→

file.content:
---
description: Intelligent task router - analyzes your request and automatically delegates to optimal workflow (plan/prompt/meta-prompt/direct execution)
arguments:
  - name: task_description
    description: "Describe what you want to accomplish. Can be empty to check todos, or a full task description."
    required: false
---

# Start Task - Intelligent Workflow Router

Analyzes your task and automatically routes to the optimal execution strategy per CLAUDE.md's Intelligent Prompt Routing system.

## Routing Logic

**If no task description provided:**
- Run `/check-todos` to list outstanding work
- Await user selection

**If task description provided, analyze and route:**

### 1. Planning Tasks → `/create-plan`
**Keywords:** "plan", "design", "architecture", "multi-phase", "roadmap"
**Complexity:** Multi-phase features, complex architecture
**Example:** "Plan the analytics dashboard feature"

→ Invoke: `/create-plan {{task_description}}`

### 2. Decision-Making → `/consider:*` frameworks
**Keywords:** "should we", "which approach", "evaluate", "compare", "decide"
**Examples:**
- "Should we use Redux or Zustand?" → `/consider:first-principles`
- "Which features to prioritize?" → `/consider:eisenhower-matrix`
- "Is this the right architecture?" → `/consider:second-order`

→ Analyze decision type and invoke appropriate framework

### 3. Debugging Tasks → `debug-like-expert` skill
**Keywords:** "debug", "intermittent", "mysterious", "sometimes fails", "race condition"
**Example:** "Debug the intermittent calendar sync issue"

→ Invoke: `Skill(debug-like-expert)` with task description

### 4. Moderate Complexity → `/create-prompt` + `/run`
**Criteria:**
- 30min - 2hr estimated work
- 3-10 files to modify
- Clear scope but needs fresh context
**Example:** "Refactor the widget loading system"

→ Invoke: `/create-prompt {{task_description}}` then offer to `/run`

### 5. Multi-Stage Features → `/create-meta-prompt`
**Keywords:** "research then implement", "explore options then build", multi-stage
**Example:** "Research authentication options then implement the best one"

→ Invoke: `/create-meta-prompt {{task_description}}`

### 6. Simple/Direct Tasks → Execute directly
**Criteria:**
- 1-2 files
- <30min work
- Clear, straightforward
**Example:** "Fix the typo in the README"

→ Execute directly in current context

## Implementation

**Step 1: Parse Arguments**
```javascript
const taskDescription = "{{task_description}}".trim();

if (!taskDescription) {
  // No task provided - check todos
  return "/check-todos";
}
```

**Step 2: Analyze Task**

Examine task description for:
- **Keywords** indicating task type
- **Complexity signals** (file count, time estimate, scope)
- **Execution pattern** (single-stage, multi-stage, research-heavy)

**Step 3: Route to Optimal Workflow**

Based on analysis:

```javascript
// Planning tasks
if (matches("plan|design|architecture|multi-phase|roadmap")) {
  invoke("/create-plan {{task_description}}");
}

// Decision-making tasks
else if (matches("should we|which|evaluate|compare|decide")) {
  determineFramework(); // first-principles, eisenhower-matrix, etc.
  invoke("/consider:[framework] {{task_description}}");
}

// Debugging tasks
else if (matches("debug|intermittent|mysterious|race condition")) {
  invoke("Skill(debug-like-expert)");
}

// Moderate complexity - delegate to fresh context
else if (estimatedComplexity === "moderate") {
  invoke("/create-prompt {{task_description}}");
  // Offer to run after creation
}

// Multi-stage features
else if (matches("research then|explore then|multi-stage")) {
  invoke("/create-meta-prompt {{task_description}}");
}

// Simple/direct - execute in current context
else {
  executeDirectly();
}
```

**Step 4: Follow Through**

After routing:
- If created prompt/plan: offer to execute with `/run` or `/run-plan`
- If direct execution: follow standard workflow (plan → approve → implement)
- If framework: present analysis and next steps

## Examples

```bash
# Automatically routes to /create-plan
/start-task Plan the user authentication system

# Automatically routes to /consider:first-principles
/start-task Should we use WebSockets or Server-Sent Events?

# Automatically routes to debug-like-expert
/start-task Debug the intermittent widget loading failure

# Automatically routes to /create-prompt
/start-task Refactor the calendar view components

# Automatically routes to /create-meta-prompt
/start-task Research GraphQL options then implement the best approach

# Direct execution
/start-task Fix typo in README.md

# Check todos (no argument)
/start-task
```

## Notes

- **Transparent routing:** User just describes task naturally
- **Zero configuration:** Analysis and routing happen automatically
- **Optimal execution:** Each task routed to best-fit workflow
- **CLAUDE.md compliant:** Follows Intelligent Prompt Routing specification
- **Composable:** Routes to other slash commands and skills as needed

Here's what I found about Claude Code plugins, marketplaces, and MCP servers in 2025:

## Claude Code Plugins Overview

Claude Code now supports plugins: custom collections of slash commands, agents, MCP servers, and hooks that install with a single command.(no content)Plugins are now in public beta for all Claude Code users. You can install them with the /plugin command and they'll work across your terminal and VS Code.

## Plugin Marketplaces

Anyone can build and host plugins and create plugin marketplaces—curated collections where other developers can discover and install plugins. You can use plugin marketplaces to share plugins with the community, distribute approved plugins across your organization, and build on existing solutions for common development challenges.(no content)To host a marketplace, all you need is a git repository, GitHub repository, or URL with a properly formatted .claude-plugin/marketplace.json file.

### Community Marketplaces

Engineer Dan Ávila's plugin marketplace offers plugins for DevOps automation, documentation generation, project management, and testing suites, while engineer Seth Hobson has curated over 80 specialized sub-agents in his GitHub repository.(no content)One comprehensive marketplace features 140+ plugins organized into 14 specialized squads including Web Development, Data & Analytics, Marketing & Growth, Finance & Accounting, and Personal Productivity, with essential MCP servers, specialized agents, and ready-to-use skills.

## Best MCP Servers for Claude Code

There are 50+ best Claude Code extensions, addons, MCP servers, and IDE integrations available for 2025, forming a curated collection with links and descriptions.

### Top MCP Servers Include:

1. **GitHub MCP Server** - Transforms Claude Code into a comprehensive development platform by enabling direct interaction with repositories, issues, pull requests, and CI/CD workflows. This integration eliminates the friction of context switching between your terminal and GitHub's web interface.

2. **Sequential Thinking MCP** - Revolutionizes how Claude Code approaches complex problem-solving by introducing a structured, reflective thinking process that mirrors human cognitive patterns.

3. **Playwright MCP Server** - Revolutionizes web automation and testing by providing Claude Code with the ability to interact with web applications using structured accessibility trees. Essential for developers building end-to-end tests, automating web workflows, or debugging complex web applications.

4. **Claude Context MCP** - An MCP plugin that adds semantic code search to Claude Code, giving it deep context from your entire codebase. It uses semantic search to find all relevant code from millions of lines.

5. **File System MCP** - Provides comprehensive local file management capabilities, enabling AI assistants to read, write, and organize files with granular permission controls. This transforms how developers interact with their file systems.

6. **Other Popular Servers** - Include Puppeteer MCP for web automation, PostgreSQL MCP for database queries, Notion MCP for productivity, Memory Bank MCP for context retention, and Figma MCP for design-to-code workflows.

## MCP Market Growth

According to Pulse data, MCP server downloads grew from just under 100,000 in November 2024 to 8 million by April 2025.(no content)A market report by Dimension Market Research projects the global MCP Server market to grow from $2,713.90 million in 2025 to $5,565.60 million by 2034, at a CAGR of 8.30%.

# Comprehensive .claude Folder Analysis & Action Plan

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Your `.claude` folder has **significant duplication and project-specific content** that needs consolidation. I found:
- **~200+ duplicate files** across all directories
- **4 project-specific automation guides** (tdr.portfolio, tjn.portfolio, system.knowledgebase)
- **16 security warning state files** (can be deleted)
- **~86MB of debug logs** (can be cleaned)
- **Multiple project-specific skills** that need genericization

---

## SECTION 1: FILES TO DELETE (Duplicates & Outdated)

### 1.1 Root Level Duplicates
| Keep | Delete |
|------|--------|
| `automation-guide.md` | `automation-guide (2).md`, `automation-guide (3).md`, `automation-guide (4).md` |
| `workflow-checklist.md` | `workflow-checklist (2).md`, `workflow-checklist (3).md`, `workflow-checklist (4).md` |
| `settings.json` | `settings (2).json`, `settings (3).json`, `settings (4).json`, `settings (5).json` |
| `settings.local.json` | `settings.local (2).json`, `settings.local (3).json`, `settings.local (4).json`, `settings.local (5).json`, `settings.local (6).json` |

### 1.2 Commands Duplicates

|------|--------|
| `commands/run.md` | `commands/run (2).md`, `commands/run (3).md`, `commands/run (4).md` |
| `commands/start-task.md` | `commands/start-task (2).md`, `commands/start-task (3).md`, `commands/start-task (4).md` |

### 1.3 Templates Duplicates
| Keep | Delete |
|------|--------|
| `templates/task-template.md` | `templates/task-template (2).md`, `templates/task-template (3).md`, `templates/task-template (4).md` |

### 1.4 Skills Duplicates (MASSIVE - Each skill has 3-4 copies of every file)
**Skills with duplicates to clean:**
- `codebase-documenter/` - 4x copies of index.js, package.json, templates
- `document-skills/` - 4x copies of index.js, package.json, all reference files
- `frontend-enhancer/` - 4x copies of index.js, package.json, assets, references
- `tech-debt-analyzer/` - 4x copies of index.js, package.json, scripts, assets
- `test-specialist/` - 4x copies of index.js, package.json, references
- `skills/README.md` - 4 copies (README.md through README (4).md)

### 1.5 Cleanup Items (Safe to Delete)
| Type | Files | Size |
|------|-------|------|
| Security warning states | 16 `security_warnings_state_*.json` files | ~4KB |
| Debug logs | ~200+ files in `debug/` | ~86MB |
| File history | ~43 session folders in `file-history/` | Large |
| Shell snapshots | Multiple `.sh` files in `shell-snapshots/` | Small |
| Old todos | Multiple agent todo files in `todos/` | Small |

---

## SECTION 2: FILES TO MERGE & MAKE GENERIC

### 2.1 Automation Guide - Needs Generic Version
**Current state:** 4 versions specific to:
- `tdr.portfolio` (thedeadrobot.com)
- `tjn.portfolio` (Travis J. Neuman)
- `system.knowledgebase`

**Action:** Create ONE generic `automation-guide.md` that works for ANY project:
- Remove project-specific paths (`tdr.portfolio/`, `tjn.portfolio/`)
- Use placeholders like `[project-name]`, `[your-project]/`
- Remove specific tech stack references (keep generic)
- Consolidate best practices from all 4 versions

### 2.2 Workflow Checklist - Needs Generic Version
**Current state:** Project-specific with:
- "tdr.portfolio" references
- "Rules #1-15" vs "Rules #1-11" inconsistency
- Project-specific testing checklists

**Action:** Create ONE generic version with:
- Placeholder project names
- Universal rule set (decide on #1-11 or #1-15)
- Configurable testing checklists

### 2.3 Project-Specific Skills - Need Evaluation
**Skills that are project-specific:**
| Skill | Project | Action |
|-------|---------|--------|
| `tdr-code-reviewer` | tdr.portfolio | Genericize or delete |
| `tdr-design-system` | tdr.portfolio | Genericize or delete |
| `tdr-feature-developer` | tdr.portfolio | Genericize or delete |

| `neuman-brain-*` | neuman-brain project | Genericize or delete |
| `lazy-golfing-*` | lazy-golfing project | Genericize or delete |

**Skills that are generic (KEEP):**
- `codebase-documenter` - General purpose
- `document-skills` - Office document handling
- `frontend-enhancer` - UI/UX enhancement
- `tech-debt-analyzer` - Code quality
- `test-specialist` - Testing
- `seo-analytics-auditor` - SEO analysis

---

## SECTION 3: WHAT TO KEEP (Essential Files)

### 3.1 Core Configuration
- `settings.json` - Main settings with plugins enabled
- `settings.local.json` - Local permission overrides
- `.credentials.json` - Authentication (DO NOT DELETE)
- `history.jsonl` - Session history
- `stats-cache.json` - Usage statistics

### 3.2 Essential Directories
- `commands/` - Slash commands (after deduplication)
- `skills/` - Agent skills (after deduplication)
- `templates/` - Task templates (after deduplication)
- `plugins/` - Installed plugins
- `plans/` - Planning files
- `projects/` - Project-specific data
- `ide/` - IDE integration
- `.claude/` - Nested Claude config

### 3.3 Good Plugins Already Enabled
Your `settings.json` shows these good plugins:
- `agent-sdk-dev@claude-code-plugins`
- `pr-review-toolkit@claude-code-plugins`
- `commit-commands@claude-code-plugins`

- `taches-cc-resources@taches-cc-resources`

---

## SECTION 4: RECOMMENDED ADDITIONS

### 4.1 High-Value MCP Servers to Add
Based on research from [MCPcat](https://mcpcat.io/guides/best-mcp-servers-for-claude-code/) and [Claude Marketplaces](https://claudemarketplaces.com/):

| MCP Server | Purpose | Priority |
|------------|---------|----------|
| **GitHub MCP** | Direct repo interaction, PR management | HIGH |
| **Playwright MCP** | Web automation, E2E testing | HIGH |
| **Sequential Thinking MCP** | Structured problem-solving | HIGH |
| **File System MCP** | Enhanced file operations | MEDIUM |
| **PostgreSQL/SQLite MCP** | Database interactions | MEDIUM |
| **Notion MCP** | Productivity integration | LOW |
| **Figma MCP** | Design-to-code workflows | LOW |

### 4.2 Recommended Plugins to Add
From [Claude Code Marketplace](https://claudecodemarketplace.com/) and [Awesome Claude Code](https://awesomeclaude.ai/):

| Plugin | Description | Priority |
|--------|-------------|----------|
| **claude-context** | Semantic code search across entire codebase | HIGH |
| **ccpm** | Project management with parallel agent execution | HIGH |
| **tdd-guard** | Automated TDD enforcement | MEDIUM |
| **zcf** | Zero-Config Code Flow automation | MEDIUM |
| **spec-flow** | Specification-driven development | MEDIUM |

### 4.3 Useful Skills to Add
| Skill | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| **API Integration Specialist** | REST/GraphQL API development |
| **Database Schema Designer** | Database design patterns |
| **Security Auditor** | Security vulnerability scanning |
| **Performance Optimizer** | Performance profiling |
| **Mobile App Developer** | React Native/Flutter guidance |
| **Desktop App Developer** | Electron/Tauri guidance |

---

## SECTION 5: ACTION PLAN

### Phase 1: Cleanup (Safe Deletions)
```
DELETE: 16 security_warnings_state_*.json files
DELETE: All (2), (3), (4) duplicate files
CLEAN: debug/ folder (keep recent, delete old)
CLEAN: file-history/ folder (optional)
CLEAN: shell-snapshots/ folder
CLEAN: todos/ folder (old agent todos)
```

### Phase 2: Merge Duplicates
```
MERGE: automation-guide files → single generic version
MERGE: workflow-checklist files → single generic version
MERGE: skills README files → single README.md
MERGE: Each skill's duplicate files → single version
```

### Phase 3: Genericize Project-Specific Content
```
UPDATE: automation-guide.md (remove tdr/tjn/system.knowledgebase refs)
UPDATE: workflow-checklist.md (remove project-specific refs)
EVALUATE: tdr-*, neuman-brain-*, lazy-golfing-* skills (genericize or remove)
```

### Phase 4: Add New Capabilities
```
INSTALL: GitHub MCP server
INSTALL: Playwright MCP server
INSTALL: Sequential Thinking MCP
INSTALL: claude-context plugin
CREATE: Generic project workflow skill
```

---

## ESTIMATED CLEANUP IMPACT

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Total files | ~400+ | ~100 |
| Duplicate files | ~200 | 0 |
| Debug logs | ~86MB | ~5MB |
| Project-specific content | 4 projects | Generic |
| Skills | 17 (many project-specific) | ~10 (all generic) |

    22→## Overview
    23→
    24→This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md rules #1-11 by:
    25→
    26→✅ **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
    27→✅ **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
    28→✅ **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
    29→✅ **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists
    30→
    31→**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.
    32→
    33→---
    34→
    35→## Quick Start
    36→
    37→### For Developers
    38→
    39→1. **Before starting any task:**
    40→   ```bash
    41→   # Check workflow checklist
    42→   cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
    43→   ```
    44→
    45→2. **Create task file from template:**
    46→   ```bash
    47→   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
    48→   ```
    49→
    50→3. **Git hook automatically enforces:**
    51→   - Task file must be staged when committing code changes
    52→   - Will block commit if missing
    53→
    54→### For Claude Code
    55→
    56→1. **Use `/start-task` command** (if available)
    57→2. **Reference workflow checklist** before starting
    58→3. **Use task template** for all new tasks
    59→4. **Git hook will catch** if task file missing
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→## Automation Tools
    64→
    65→### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook
    66→
    67→**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
    68→**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
    69→**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7
    70→
    71→**How it works:**
    72→1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
    73→2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
    74→3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
    75→4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)
    76→
    77→**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
    78→```
    79→━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    80→  ❌ COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
    81→━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
    82→
    83→Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.
    84→
    85→Changed code files:
    86→  - src/components/Modal.tsx
    87→  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
    88→
    89→Required Action:
    90→  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
    91→  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
    92→  3. Retry commit: git commit
    93→```
    94→
    95→**Emergency Bypass:**
    96→```bash
    97→git commit --no-verify
    98→```
    99→⚠️ **Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies
   100→

   188→## Setup Instructions
   189→
   190→### Initial Setup (One-Time)
   191→
   192→The automation tools are already set up in this repository. No additional installation required.
   193→
   194→**Verify setup:**
   195→```bash
   196→# Check git hook exists and is executable
   197→ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   198→
   199→# Check automation files exist
   200→ls -la .claude/
   201→
   202→# Expected output:
   203→# .claude/
   204→# ├── automation-guide.md         (this file)
   205→# ├── workflow-checklist.md       (step-by-step checklist)
   206→# ├── templates/
   207→# │   └── task-template.md        (task file template)
   208→# └── commands/
   209→#     └── start-task.md           (custom command)
   210→```
   211→
   212→### For New Developers
   213→
   214→1. **Clone repository**
   215→2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (especially rules #1-11)
   216→3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
   217→4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   218→5. **Test git hook:**
   219→   ```bash
   220→   # Make a small change to src/ file
   221→   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx
   222→
   223→   # Try to commit without staging task file
   224→   git add src/main.tsx
   225→   git commit -m "test"
   226→   # Should be BLOCKED
   227→
   228→   # Stage task file and retry
   229→   git add tasks/some-task.md
   230→   git commit -m "test"
   231→   # Should succeed
   232→
   233→   # Revert test
   234→   git reset HEAD~1
   235→   git checkout src/main.tsx
   236→   ```
