   239→
   240→## How It Works
   241→
   242→### Git Hook Workflow
   243→
   244→```
   245→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   246→│  Developer runs: git commit                                  │
   247→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   248→                     │
   249→                     ▼
   250→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   251→│  Pre-commit hook executes (.git/hooks/pre-commit)           │
   252→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   253→                     │
   254→                     ▼
   255→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   256→│  Check: Are any src/ files staged?                          │
   257→│  (src/, components/, widgets/, stores/, services/)          │
   258→└────────────┬────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┘
   259→             │ YES                    │ NO
   260→             ▼                        ▼
   261→┌──────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
   262→│ Check: Is tasks/*.md     │  │ Allow commit                 │
   263→│ file staged?             │  │ (doc-only, config, etc.)     │
   264→└──────┬───────────┬───────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
   265→       │ YES       │ NO
   266→       ▼           ▼
   267→┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   268→│ ✅ Allow   │  │ ❌ BLOCK commit                             │
   269→│ commit     │  │ Display error message with instructions     │
   270→└────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   271→```
   272→
   273→### Task File Lifecycle
   274→
   275→```
   276→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   277→│  1. Start Task                                               │
   278→│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
   279→│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
   280→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   281→                     │
   282→                     ▼
   283→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   284→│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
   285→│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
   286→│     - Create todo checklist with time estimates             │
   287→│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
   288→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   289→                     │
   290→                     ▼
   291→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   292→│  3. Implementation                                           │
   293→│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
   294→│     - Add implementation notes                               │
   295→│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
   296→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   297→                     │
   298→                     ▼
   299→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   300→│  4. Completion                                               │
   301→│     - Fill out review section                                │
   302→│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
   303→│     - Add testing status, time comparison                    │
   304→│     - Update docs/ if architecture changed                   │
   305→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   306→                     │
   307→                     ▼
   308→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   309→│  5. Commit                                                   │
   310→│     - Stage code files: git add src/                        │
   311→│     - Stage task file: git add tasks/[feature].md          │
   312→│     - Pre-commit hook verifies both staged                   │
   313→│     - Commit succeeds ✅                                     │
   314→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   315→```
   316→

   318→
   319→## Troubleshooting
   320→
   321→### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running
   322→
   323→**Symptoms:**
   324→- Can commit code without task file
   325→- No error message appears
   326→
   327→**Causes & Fixes:**
   328→
   329→**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
   330→```bash
   331→# Fix: Make it executable
   332→chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
   333→```
   334→
   335→**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
   336→```bash
   337→# Check if file exists
   338→ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   339→
   340→# If missing, recreate from template (if you have one)
   341→# Or re-run setup script
   342→```
   343→
   344→**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
   345→```bash
   346→# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
   347→git commit  # ✅ Correct
   348→git commit --no-verify  # ❌ Bypasses hook
   349→```
   350→
   351→**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
   352→```bash
   353→# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe)
   354→# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
   355→```
   356→
   357→---
   358→
   359→### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits
   360→
   361→**Symptoms:**
   362→- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
   363→- False positive error
   364→
   365→**Causes & Fixes:**
   366→
   367→**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
   368→```bash
   369→# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
   370→git add tasks/my-feature.md  # ✅ Correct
   371→git add my-feature.md         # ❌ Wrong location
   372→git add docs/task.md          # ❌ Wrong location
   373→```
   374→
   375→**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
   376→```bash
   377→# Check what's staged
   378→git diff --cached --name-only
   379→
   380→# Ensure task file is in the list
   381→# If not, stage it:
   382→git add tasks/my-feature.md
   383→```
   384→
   385→**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
   386→```bash
   387→# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
   388→git add tasks/my-feature.md   # ✅ Correct
   389→git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # ❌ Wrong extension
   390→```
   391→
   392→---
   393→
   394→### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template
   395→
   396→**Symptoms:**
   397→- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
   398→- File not found error
   399→
   400→**Fixes:**
   401→```bash
   402→# Check if template exists
   403→ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md
   404→
   405→# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
   406→ls -la .claude/
   407→
   408→# Ensure you're in repository root
   409→pwd  # Should end with system.knowledgebase
   410→```
   411→
   412→---
   413→
   414→### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File
   415→
   416→**Symptoms:**
   417→- Code commits go through without task file
   418→- Hook seems to not detect code changes
   419→
   420→**Causes & Fixes:**
   421→
   422→**Cause 1:** Code not in src/ directory
   423→```bash
   424→# Hook only checks src/ directory
   425→# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it
   426→
   427→# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
   428→# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
   429→# Find line: CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/')
   430→# Add more patterns: grep -E '^(src/|lib/|components/)'
   431→```
   432→
   433→**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
   434→```bash
   435→# Hook ignores .md files in src/
   436→# (to allow README updates without task file)
   437→
   438→# This is intentional behavior
   439→```
   440→
   441→---
   442→
   443→### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying
   444→
   445→**Symptoms:**
   446→- Commit blocked but no error message shown
   447→- Silent failure
   448→
   449→**Fixes:**
   450→```bash
   451→# Test hook manually
   452→bash .git/hooks/pre-commit
   453→
   454→# Check for syntax errors
   455→bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit
   456→
   457→# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
   458→# Some terminals don't support color codes
   459→```
   460→
   461→---

   463→## Customization
   464→
   465→### Adding More File Patterns to Hook
   466→
   467→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)
   468→
   469→```bash
   470→# Current pattern (only src/)
   471→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')
   472→
   473→# Add more directories (lib/, config/, etc.)
   474→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
   475→```
   476→
   477→### Excluding Specific Files from Hook
   478→
   479→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)
   480→
   481→```bash
   482→# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
   483→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$)')
   484→```
   485→
   486→### Customizing Task Template
   487→
   488→**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   489→
   490→Add/remove sections as needed:
   491→- Additional checklists
   492→- Project-specific requirements
   493→- Custom metadata fields
   494→
   495→**Example additions:**
   496→```markdown
   497→## Security Checklist
   498→- [ ] Input validation added
   499→- [ ] XSS prevention verified
   500→- [ ] SQL injection prevented
   501→
   502→## Performance Checklist
   503→- [ ] Bundle size impact < 10KB
   504→- [ ] No memory leaks
   505→- [ ] Animations 60fps
   506→```
   507→
   508→### Adding New Custom Commands
   509→
   510→**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`
   511→
   512→Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`
   513→
   514→```markdown
   515→# Review Task Checklist
   516→
   517→Before marking task complete:
   518→
   519→- [ ] Review section filled out
   520→- [ ] All files documented
   521→- [ ] Testing complete
   522→- [ ] Documentation updated
   523→- [ ] Bundle size checked
   524→```
   525→
   526→### Adjusting Hook Error Messages
   527→
   528→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Lines 30-70)
   529→
   530→Customize:
   531→- Error message text
   532→- Color scheme (ANSI codes)
   533→- Instructions provided
   534→- Examples shown

   537→
   538→## Best Practices
   539→
   540→### 1. Always Start with the Checklist
   541→
   542→Before starting any task:
   543→```bash
   544→# Read the workflow checklist
   545→cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   546→
   547→# Or use custom command
   548→/start-task  # If available in Claude Code
   549→```
   550→
   551→### 2. Create Task File FIRST
   552→
   553→```bash
   554→# ✅ CORRECT ORDER
   555→cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
   556→# Fill out the task file
   557→# THEN start coding
   558→
   559→# ❌ WRONG ORDER
   560→# Start coding first
   561→# Create task file later (will forget details!)
   562→```
   563→
   564→### 3. Update Task File as You Work
   565→
   566→```markdown
   567→## Todo Checklist
   568→
   569→### Phase 1
   570→- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE (took 30 min)
   571→- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
   572→- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for user input)
   573→```
   574→
   575→Keep the task file up to date, don't batch updates at the end.
   576→
   577→### 4. Fill Out Review Section Immediately
   578→
   579→When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
   580→- Summary of changes
   581→- Files created/modified
   582→- Testing status
   583→- Issues encountered
   584→
   585→Don't wait days/weeks - you'll forget important details!
   586→
   587→### 5. Stage Task File with Code
   588→
   589→```bash
   590→# ✅ CORRECT - Stage both together
   591→git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   592→git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
   593→git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"
   594→
   595→# ❌ WRONG - Separate commits
   596→git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   597→git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
   598→# Oops, forgot task file! (Hook will block this)
   599→```
   600→
   601→### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names
   602→
   603→```bash
   604→# ✅ GOOD - Descriptive, specific
   605→tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
   606→tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
   607→tasks/refactor-zustand-stores.md
   608→
   609→# ❌ BAD - Vague, generic
   610→tasks/todo.md
   611→tasks/task1.md
   612→tasks/stuff.md
   613→tasks/changes.md
   614→```
   615→
   616→### 7. One Task File Per Feature
   617→
   618→Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.
   619→
   620→```bash
   621→# ✅ CORRECT - Separate files
   622→tasks/add-search-feature.md
   623→tasks/add-export-feature.md
   624→
   625→# ❌ WRONG - One giant file
   626→tasks/all-features.md
   627→```
   628→
   629→### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES
   630→
   631→```bash
   632→# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
   633→git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
   634→git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
   635→git commit --no-verify  # Doc-only changes (maybe)
   636→
   637→# NOT for:
   638→git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
   639→git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later" (you won't!)
   640→```
   641→
   642→### 9. Keep Documentation Updated
   643→
   644→If architecture changes, update docs:
   645→```bash
   646→git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
   647→git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
   648→git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md  # New architecture doc
   649→git commit -m "feat: Add undo system
   650→
   651→Closes #42"
   652→```
   653→
   654→### 10. Reference CLAUDE.md Rules
   655→
   656→When in doubt, check CLAUDE.md:
   657→```bash
   658→# Quick reference
   659→cat CLAUDE.md | grep "^[0-9])"
   660→
   661→# Full read
   662→cat CLAUDE.md
   663→```

   666→
   667→## Integration with Claude Code
   668→
   669→### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code
   670→
   671→1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
   672→2. **Use task template** for all planning
   673→3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
   674→4. **Fill review section** when done
   675→5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged
   676→
   677→### Behavioral Changes for Claude Code
   678→
   679→**Before starting ANY task:**
   680→- ✅ Read `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   681→- ✅ Create task file from template
   682→- ✅ Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)
   683→
   684→**During implementation:**
   685→- ✅ Update todos as you complete them
   686→- ✅ Document decisions in task file
   687→- ✅ Track time estimates vs actual
   688→
   689→**When complete:**
   690→- ✅ Fill out review section
   691→- ✅ Update docs/ if architecture changed
   692→- ✅ Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)
   693→
   694→**On every commit:**
   695→- ✅ Stage code files AND task file together
   696→- ✅ Git hook verifies compliance
   697→- ✅ Commit proceeds
   698→
   699→---
   700→
   701→## Appendix
   702→
   703→### File Structure Reference
   704→
   705→```
   706→system.knowledgebase/
   707→├── .git/
   708→│   └── hooks/
   709→│       └── pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
   710→├── .claude/
   711→│   ├── automation-guide.md         # This file
   712→│   ├── workflow-checklist.md       # Step-by-step checklist
   713→│   ├── templates/
   714→│   │   └── task-template.md        # Task file template
   715→│   └── commands/
   716→│       └── start-task.md           # Custom command
   717→├── tasks/
   718→│   └── [feature-name].md           # Task files (one per feature)
   719→├── CLAUDE.md                        # Main workflow guide (rules #1-11)
   720→└── README.md                        # User-facing documentation
   721→```
   722→
   723→### Git Hook Exit Codes
   724→
   725→- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
   726→- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)
   727→
   728→### ANSI Color Codes Used
   729→
   730→```bash
   731→RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
   732→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
   733→GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
   734→BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
   735→NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
   736→```
   737→
   738→---
   739→
   740→## Support & Questions
   741→
   742→**For questions about:**
   743→- **CLAUDE.md workflow:** See `CLAUDE.md` in root
   744→- **Workflow steps:** See `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   745→- **Task templates:** See `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   746→- **Git hook issues:** See troubleshooting section above
   747→- **Feature requests:** Create GitHub issue
   748→
   749→---
   750→
   751→**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
   752→**Maintained By:** Engineering Team

   754→

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide

**Version:** 1.0
**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview](#overview)
2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
7. [Customization](#customization)
8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)

---

## Overview

This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md rules #1-11 by:

✅ **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
✅ **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
✅ **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
✅ **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists

**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.

---

## Quick Start

### For Developers

1. **Before starting any task:**
   ```bash
   # Check workflow checklist
   cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   ```

2. **Create task file from template:**
   ```bash
   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   ```

3. **Git hook automatically enforces:**
   - Task file must be staged when committing code changes
   - Will block commit if missing

### For Claude Code

1. **Use `/start-task` command** (if available)
2. **Reference workflow checklist** before starting
3. **Use task template** for all new tasks
4. **Git hook will catch** if task file missing

---

## Automation Tools

### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook

**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7

**How it works:**
1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)

**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  ❌ COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.

Changed code files:
  - src/components/Modal.tsx
  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts

Required Action:
  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
  3. Retry commit: git commit
```

**Emergency Bypass:**
```bash
git commit --no-verify
```
⚠️ **Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies

---

### 2. Workflow Checklist

**Location:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
**Enforces:** All rules #1-11

**When to use:**
- Before starting ANY task
- During implementation (reference as needed)
- Before marking work complete
- Before creating git commits

**Sections:**
- ✅ Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
- ✅ During Implementation
- ✅ When Complete
- ✅ Git Commit Checklist
- ✅ Rules #1-11 Quick Reference

**View checklist:**
```bash
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
```

---

### 3. Task Template

**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7

**Sections included:**
- Overview and problem statement
- Files to read
- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
- Todo checklist with time estimates
- Design considerations
- Implementation notes
- Testing checklist
- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)

**Create new task:**
```bash
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Good naming examples:**
- ✅ `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
- ✅ `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
- ✅ `tasks/notes-ux-improvements.md`

**Bad naming examples:**
- ❌ `tasks/todo.md`
- ❌ `tasks/task1.md`
- ❌ `tasks/new.md`

---

### 4. Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow reminders

#### `/start-task` Command

**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
**Purpose:** Comprehensive reminder before starting any task

**What it covers:**
1. Sync with main branch (Rule #11)
2. Read CLAUDE.md (Rules #1-11)
3. Create task file from template
4. Fill out task plan
5. Get user approval (Rule #3)
6. Implementation workflow
7. Review section and documentation updates

**Use when:**
- Starting a new task
- Unsure of the workflow
- Need a quick reference

---

## Setup Instructions

### Initial Setup (One-Time)

The automation tools are already set up in this repository. No additional installation required.

**Verify setup:**
```bash
# Check git hook exists and is executable
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check automation files exist
ls -la .claude/

# Expected output:
# .claude/
# ├── automation-guide.md         (this file)
# ├── workflow-checklist.md       (step-by-step checklist)
# ├── templates/
# │   └── task-template.md        (task file template)
# └── commands/
#     └── start-task.md           (custom command)
```

### For New Developers

1. **Clone repository**
2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (especially rules #1-11)
3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
5. **Test git hook:**
   ```bash
   # Make a small change to src/ file
   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx

   # Try to commit without staging task file
   git add src/main.tsx
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should be BLOCKED

   # Stage task file and retry
   git add tasks/some-task.md
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should succeed

   # Revert test
   git reset HEAD~1
   git checkout src/main.tsx
   ```

---

## How It Works

### Git Hook Workflow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Developer runs: git commit                                  │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Pre-commit hook executes (.git/hooks/pre-commit)           │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Check: Are any src/ files staged?                          │
│  (src/, components/, widgets/, stores/, services/)          │
└────────────┬────────────────────────┬────────────────────────┘
             │ YES                    │ NO
             ▼                        ▼
┌──────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ Check: Is tasks/*.md     │  │ Allow commit                 │
│ file staged?             │  │ (doc-only, config, etc.)     │
└──────┬───────────┬───────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
       │ YES       │ NO
       ▼           ▼
┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ✅ Allow   │  │ ❌ BLOCK commit                             │
│ commit     │  │ Display error message with instructions     │
└────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Task File Lifecycle

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. Start Task                                               │
│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
│     - Create todo checklist with time estimates             │
│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  3. Implementation                                           │
│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
│     - Add implementation notes                               │
│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  4. Completion                                               │
│     - Fill out review section                                │
│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
│     - Add testing status, time comparison                    │
│     - Update docs/ if architecture changed                   │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  5. Commit                                                   │
│     - Stage code files: git add src/                        │
│     - Stage task file: git add tasks/[feature].md          │
│     - Pre-commit hook verifies both staged                   │
│     - Commit succeeds ✅                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Troubleshooting

### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running

**Symptoms:**
- Can commit code without task file
- No error message appears

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
```bash
# Fix: Make it executable
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
```

**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
```bash
# Check if file exists
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# If missing, recreate from template (if you have one)
# Or re-run setup script
```

**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
```bash
# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
git commit  # ✅ Correct
git commit --no-verify  # ❌ Bypasses hook
```

**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
```bash
# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe)
# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
```

---

### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits

**Symptoms:**
- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
- False positive error

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
```bash
# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
git add tasks/my-feature.md  # ✅ Correct
git add my-feature.md         # ❌ Wrong location
git add docs/task.md          # ❌ Wrong location
```

**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
```bash
# Check what's staged
git diff --cached --name-only

# Ensure task file is in the list
# If not, stage it:
git add tasks/my-feature.md
```

**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
```bash
# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
git add tasks/my-feature.md   # ✅ Correct
git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # ❌ Wrong extension
```

---

### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template

**Symptoms:**
- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
- File not found error

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Check if template exists
ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md

# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
ls -la .claude/

# Ensure you're in repository root
pwd  # Should end with system.knowledgebase
```

---

### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File

**Symptoms:**
- Code commits go through without task file
- Hook seems to not detect code changes

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Code not in src/ directory
```bash
# Hook only checks src/ directory
# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it

# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
# Find line: CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/')
# Add more patterns: grep -E '^(src/|lib/|components/)'
```

**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
```bash
# Hook ignores .md files in src/
# (to allow README updates without task file)

# This is intentional behavior
```

---

### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying

**Symptoms:**
- Commit blocked but no error message shown
- Silent failure

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Test hook manually
bash .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check for syntax errors
bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
# Some terminals don't support color codes
```

---

## Customization

### Adding More File Patterns to Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)

```bash
# Current pattern (only src/)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')

# Add more directories (lib/, config/, etc.)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
```

### Excluding Specific Files from Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Line ~20)

```bash
# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$)')
```

### Customizing Task Template

**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`

Add/remove sections as needed:
- Additional checklists
- Project-specific requirements
- Custom metadata fields

**Example additions:**
```markdown
## Security Checklist
- [ ] Input validation added
- [ ] XSS prevention verified
- [ ] SQL injection prevented

## Performance Checklist
- [ ] Bundle size impact < 10KB
- [ ] No memory leaks
- [ ] Animations 60fps
```

### Adding New Custom Commands

**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`

Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`

```markdown
# Review Task Checklist

Before marking task complete:

- [ ] Review section filled out
- [ ] All files documented
- [ ] Testing complete
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Bundle size checked
```

### Adjusting Hook Error Messages

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (Lines 30-70)

Customize:
- Error message text
- Color scheme (ANSI codes)
- Instructions provided
- Examples shown

---

## Best Practices

### 1. Always Start with the Checklist

Before starting any task:
```bash
# Read the workflow checklist
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md

# Or use custom command
/start-task  # If available in Claude Code
```

### 2. Create Task File FIRST

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT ORDER
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
# Fill out the task file
# THEN start coding

# ❌ WRONG ORDER
# Start coding first
# Create task file later (will forget details!)
```

### 3. Update Task File as You Work

```markdown
## Todo Checklist

### Phase 1
- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE (took 30 min)
- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for user input)
```

Keep the task file up to date, don't batch updates at the end.

### 4. Fill Out Review Section Immediately

When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
- Summary of changes
- Files created/modified
- Testing status
- Issues encountered

Don't wait days/weeks - you'll forget important details!

### 5. Stage Task File with Code

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT - Stage both together
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"

# ❌ WRONG - Separate commits
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
# Oops, forgot task file! (Hook will block this)
```

### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names

```bash
# ✅ GOOD - Descriptive, specific
tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
tasks/refactor-zustand-stores.md

# ❌ BAD - Vague, generic
tasks/todo.md
tasks/task1.md
tasks/stuff.md
tasks/changes.md
```

### 7. One Task File Per Feature

Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.

```bash
# ✅ CORRECT - Separate files
tasks/add-search-feature.md
tasks/add-export-feature.md

# ❌ WRONG - One giant file
tasks/all-features.md
```

### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES

```bash
# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
git commit --no-verify  # Doc-only changes (maybe)

# NOT for:
git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later" (you won't!)
```

### 9. Keep Documentation Updated

If architecture changes, update docs:
```bash
git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md  # New architecture doc
git commit -m "feat: Add undo system

Closes #42"
```

### 10. Reference CLAUDE.md Rules

When in doubt, check CLAUDE.md:
```bash
# Quick reference
cat CLAUDE.md | grep "^[0-9])"

# Full read
cat CLAUDE.md
```

---

## Integration with Claude Code

### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code

1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
2. **Use task template** for all planning
3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
4. **Fill review section** when done
5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged

### Behavioral Changes for Claude Code

**Before starting ANY task:**
- ✅ Read `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
- ✅ Create task file from template
- ✅ Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)

**During implementation:**
- ✅ Update todos as you complete them
- ✅ Document decisions in task file
- ✅ Track time estimates vs actual

**When complete:**
- ✅ Fill out review section
- ✅ Update docs/ if architecture changed
- ✅ Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)

**On every commit:**
- ✅ Stage code files AND task file together
- ✅ Git hook verifies compliance
- ✅ Commit proceeds

---

## Appendix

### File Structure Reference

```
system.knowledgebase/
├── .git/
│   └── hooks/
│       └── pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
├── .claude/
│   ├── automation-guide.md         # This file
│   ├── workflow-checklist.md       # Step-by-step checklist
│   ├── templates/
│   │   └── task-template.md        # Task file template
│   └── commands/
│       └── start-task.md           # Custom command
├── tasks/
│   └── [feature-name].md           # Task files (one per feature)
├── CLAUDE.md                        # Main workflow guide (rules #1-11)
└── README.md                        # User-facing documentation
```

### Git Hook Exit Codes

- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)

### ANSI Color Codes Used

```bash
RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
```

---

## Support & Questions

**For questions about:**
- **CLAUDE.md workflow:** See `CLAUDE.md` in root
- **Workflow steps:** See `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
- **Task templates:** See `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
- **Git hook issues:** See troubleshooting section above
- **Feature requests:** Create GitHub issue

---

**Last Updated:** November 14, 2025
**Maintained By:** Engineering Team
**Version:** 1.0

**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. They catch mistakes BEFORE they become problems. Follow the workflow, and your development process will be smoother and more consistent.

input.content:
# CLAUDE.md Automation Guide

**Version:** 3.0 (Merged & Generic)
**Last Updated:** December 2025
**Purpose:** Comprehensive guide for CLAUDE.md compliance automation tools - works with ANY project

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Overview](#overview)
2. [Quick Start](#quick-start)
3. [Automation Tools](#automation-tools)
4. [Setup Instructions](#setup-instructions)
5. [How It Works](#how-it-works)
6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
7. [Customization](#customization)
8. [Best Practices](#best-practices)
9. [Project-Specific Configurations](#project-specific-configurations)

---

## Overview

This automation system ensures compliance with CLAUDE.md workflow rules by:

- **Preventing commits** without task file updates (Rule #1, #2, #7)
- **Providing templates** for consistent task documentation (Rule #1)
- **Reminding workflows** before starting tasks (Rule #3)
- **Enforcing standards** through git hooks and checklists

**Core Principle:** Make it harder to forget than to remember.

### Supported Project Types

This guide supports multiple project configurations:

| Project Type | Tech Stack | Build Tools |
|--------------|------------|-------------|
| **Static Sites** | Pure HTML/CSS/JS | None (direct browser) |
| **React/Vue/Angular** | TypeScript/JavaScript | npm/vite/webpack |
| **Full-Stack** | Any framework | npm + backend tools |
| **Automation** | Python/Node.js | pip/npm |

---

## Quick Start

### Before Starting ANY Task

1. **Sync with main branch:**
   ```bash
   # For main branch
   git fetch origin main && git merge origin/main --no-edit

   # For master branch
   git fetch origin master && git merge origin/master --no-edit
   ```

2. **Check workflow checklist:**
   ```bash
   cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   ```

3. **Create task file from template:**
   ```bash
   cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
   ```

4. **Get user approval (Rule #3):**
   Use `ExitPlanMode` tool or ask user before coding

### For Static Sites (HTML/CSS/JS)

```bash
# Start local server
python3 -m http.server 8000
# Or: npx serve .
# Visit: http://localhost:8000
```

### For Node.js/React Projects

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Start development server
npm run dev

# Run tests
npm run test

# Type check (TypeScript)
npm run type-check

# Build
npm run build
```

### For Automation Scripts

```bash
# Test with dry run first
DRY_RUN=true python automation/[script].py

# Verify output before running live
```

---

## Automation Tools

### 1. Git Pre-Commit Hook

**Location:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
**Purpose:** Enforce task file updates when code changes
**Enforces:** CLAUDE.md Rules #1, #2, #7

**How it works:**
1. Detects if code files (src/, components/, etc.) are staged
2. Checks if a tasks/*.md file is also staged
3. Blocks commit if code changed but no task file staged
4. Allows commit for non-code changes (docs, config)

**Example Output (Blocked Commit):**
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  COMMIT BLOCKED - Missing Task File (CLAUDE.md Rule #1, #2, #7)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.

Changed code files:
  - src/components/Modal.tsx
  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts

Required Action:
  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
  3. Retry commit: git commit
```

**Emergency Bypass:**
```bash
git commit --no-verify
```
**Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies

---

### 2. Workflow Checklist

**Location:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
**Purpose:** Step-by-step checklist for CLAUDE.md compliance
**Enforces:** All workflow rules

**When to use:**
- Before starting ANY task
- During implementation (reference as needed)
- Before marking work complete
- Before creating git commits

**Sections:**
- Pre-Flight Checklist (before starting)
- During Implementation
- When Complete
- Git Commit Checklist
- Quick Reference for all rules

**View checklist:**
```bash
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
```

---

### 3. Task Template

**Location:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
**Purpose:** Standardized format for all task files
**Enforces:** Rules #1, #2, #7

**Sections included:**
- Overview and problem statement
- Files to read
- Proposed changes (files to create/modify)
- Todo checklist with time estimates
- Design considerations
- Implementation notes
- Testing checklist
- **Review section** (to be filled when complete)

**Create new task:**
```bash
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/[feature-name].md
```

**Good naming examples:**
- `tasks/add-search-functionality.md`
- `tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md`
- `tasks/refactor-state-management.md`
- `tasks/optimize-image-loading.md`

**Bad naming examples:**
- `tasks/todo.md`
- `tasks/task1.md`
- `tasks/new.md`
- `tasks/stuff.md`

---

### 4. Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/`
**Purpose:** Quick access to CLAUDE.md workflow

#### `/start-task` Command

**File:** `.claude/commands/start-task.md`
**Purpose:** Intelligent task router

**What it covers:**
1. Routes to appropriate workflow based on task type
2. Creates task files from templates
3. Checks existing todos
4. Initiates planning mode
5. Gets user approval before implementation

**Use when:**
- Starting a new task
- Unsure of the workflow
- Need a quick reference

#### `/run` Command

**File:** `.claude/commands/run.md`
**Purpose:** Execute prompts with auto-archiving

---

## Setup Instructions

### File Structure (Generic)

```
[project-root]/
├── .git/
│   └── hooks/
│       └── pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
├── .claude/
│   ├── automation-guide.md         (this file)
│   ├── workflow-checklist.md       (step-by-step checklist)
│   ├── settings.json               (Claude settings)
│   ├── settings.local.json         (local overrides)
│   ├── templates/
│   │   └── task-template.md        (task file template)
│   ├── commands/
│   │   └── start-task.md           (custom commands)
│   └── skills/
│       └── [skill-name]/           (custom skills)
├── tasks/                           (task files go here)
├── CLAUDE.md                        (main workflow guide)
└── README.md                        (project documentation)
```

### Initial Setup (One-Time)

**Verify setup:**
```bash
# Check git hook exists and is executable
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check automation files exist
ls -la .claude/

# Make hook executable (if needed)
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
```

### For New Developers/Contributors

1. **Clone repository**
2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules)
3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
5. **Test git hook:**
   ```bash
   # Make a small test change
   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx  # or index.js, etc.

   # Try to commit without staging task file
   git add src/main.tsx
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should be BLOCKED

   # Stage task file and retry
   git add tasks/some-task.md
   git commit -m "test"
   # Should succeed

   # Revert test
   git reset HEAD~1
   git checkout src/main.tsx
   ```

---

## How It Works

### Git Hook Workflow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Developer runs: git commit                                  │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Pre-commit hook executes (.git/hooks/pre-commit)           │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Check: Are any code files staged?                          │
│  (src/, components/, widgets/, stores/, services/, lib/)    │
└────────────┬────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
             │ YES                    │ NO
             ▼                        ▼
┌──────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ Check: Is tasks/*.md     │  │ Allow commit                 │
│ file staged?             │  │ (doc-only, config, etc.)     │
└──────┬───────────┬───────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
       │ YES       │ NO
       ▼           ▼
┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Allow      │  │ BLOCK commit                                │
│ commit     │  │ Display error message with instructions     │
└────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Task File Lifecycle

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  1. Start Task                                               │
│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
│     - Create todo checklist                                  │
│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  3. Implementation                                           │
│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
│     - Add implementation notes                               │
│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  4. Completion                                               │
│     - Fill out review section                                │
│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
│     - Add testing status                                     │
│     - Update docs/ if architecture changed                   │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  5. Commit                                                   │
│     - Stage all files: git add .                            │
│     - Write descriptive commit message                       │
│     - Push to remote: git push origin [branch]              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Troubleshooting

### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running

**Symptoms:**
- Can commit code without task file
- No error message appears

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
```bash
# Fix: Make it executable
chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
```

**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
```bash
# Check if file exists
ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit

# If missing, recreate from template
```

**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
```bash
# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
git commit  # Correct
git commit --no-verify  # Bypasses hook
```

**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
```bash
# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe or PowerShell for hooks)
# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
# Note: On Windows, you may need to use Git Bash terminal
```

---

### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits

**Symptoms:**
- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
- False positive error

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
```bash
# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
git add tasks/my-feature.md  # Correct
git add my-feature.md         # Wrong location
git add docs/task.md          # Wrong location
```

**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
```bash
# Check what's staged
git diff --cached --name-only

# Ensure task file is in the list
# If not, stage it:
git add tasks/my-feature.md
```

**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
```bash
# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
git add tasks/my-feature.md   # Correct
git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # Wrong extension
```

---

### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template

**Symptoms:**
- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
- File not found error

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Check if template exists
ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md

# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
ls -la .claude/

# Ensure you're in repository root
pwd
```

---

### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File

**Symptoms:**
- Code commits go through without task file
- Hook seems to not detect code changes

**Causes & Fixes:**

**Cause 1:** Code not in monitored directories
```bash
# Hook checks specific directories (src/, lib/, components/, etc.)
# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it

# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
# Find the grep pattern line and add more directories
```

**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
```bash
# Hook ignores .md files in src/
# (to allow README updates without task file)
# This is intentional behavior
```

---

### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying

**Symptoms:**
- Commit blocked but no error message shown
- Silent failure

**Fixes:**
```bash
# Test hook manually
bash .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Check for syntax errors
bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit

# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
# Some terminals don't support color codes
```

---

## Customization

### Adding More File Patterns to Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`

```bash
# Current pattern (example)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')

# Add more directories
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/|components/|services/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
```

### Excluding Specific Files from Hook

**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`

```bash
# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$|\.d\.ts$)')
```

### Customizing Task Template

**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`

**Example additions:**
```markdown
## Security Checklist
- [ ] Input validation added
- [ ] XSS prevention verified
- [ ] SQL injection prevented
- [ ] CSRF protection verified

## Performance Checklist
- [ ] Bundle size impact acceptable
- [ ] No memory leaks
- [ ] Animations 60fps
- [ ] Lazy loading where appropriate

## Accessibility Checklist
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Screen reader compatible
- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
- [ ] Focus indicators visible

## Browser/Device Testing
- [ ] Chrome
- [ ] Firefox
- [ ] Safari
- [ ] Edge
- [ ] Mobile (375px)
- [ ] Tablet (768px)
- [ ] Desktop (1024px+)
```

### Adding New Custom Commands

**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`

Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`
```markdown
---
description: Review task before marking complete
---

# Review Task Checklist

Before marking task complete:

- [ ] Review section filled out
- [ ] All files documented
- [ ] Testing complete
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] Bundle size checked
```

### Adding More Skills

1. Create folder: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/`
2. Create file: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/SKILL.md`
3. Follow existing skill format (YAML frontmatter + content)
4. Update `.claude/skills/README.md`

---

## Best Practices

### 1. Always Start with the Checklist

```bash
cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
# Or use custom command
/start-task
```

### 2. Create Task File FIRST

```bash
# CORRECT ORDER
cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
# Fill out the task file
# THEN start coding

# WRONG ORDER
# Start coding first
# Create task file later (will forget details!)
```

### 3. Update Task File as You Work

```markdown
## Todo Checklist

### Phase 1
- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE
- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for input)
```

### 4. Fill Review Section Immediately

When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
- Summary of changes
- Files created/modified
- Testing status
- Issues encountered

Don't wait - you'll forget important details!

### 5. Stage Task File with Code

```bash
# CORRECT - Stage both together
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"

# WRONG - Separate commits (hook will block)
git add src/components/Modal.tsx
git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
```

### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names

```bash
# GOOD - Descriptive, specific
tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
tasks/refactor-state-stores.md

# BAD - Vague, generic
tasks/todo.md
tasks/task1.md
tasks/stuff.md
```

### 7. One Task File Per Feature

Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.

### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES

```bash
# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
git commit --no-verify  # Critical security fix

# NOT for:
git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later"
```

### 9. Keep Documentation Updated

If architecture changes, update docs:
```bash
git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md
git commit -m "feat: Add undo system"
```

### 10. Screenshot Before UI Changes

For ANY visual/UI change:
1. Screenshot current state
2. Make ONE change (not multiple at once)
3. Screenshot after
4. Compare to baseline
5. Get user approval before committing

### 11. Test Automation with Dry Run

```bash
# CORRECT
DRY_RUN=true python automation/script.py
# Verify output looks good
# THEN remove DRY_RUN for real execution

# WRONG
# Just run live and hope it works
```

### 12. Meaningful Commit Messages

```bash
# GOOD
git commit -m "feat: Add YouTube social link

- Added new link to social menu
- Updated animation timing
- Tested on all browsers

Generated with Claude Code
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>"

# BAD
git commit -m "update stuff"
```

---

## Project-Specific Configurations

### Static Site Projects (HTML/CSS/JS)

- No build tools required
- Use `python3 -m http.server 8000` for local testing
- Focus on browser compatibility testing
- Consider: GIF optimization, lazy loading

### React/Vue/Angular Projects

- Run `npm run test` before commits
- Run `npm run type-check` for TypeScript
- Run `npm run lint` for code style
- Run `npm run build` to verify bundle size
- Test in light mode AND dark mode

### Full-Stack Projects

- Test both frontend and backend changes
- Verify API contracts unchanged (or documented)
- Check database migrations
- Test authentication flows

### Automation Projects

- Always use DRY_RUN=true first
- Verify API keys are properly stored
- Test error handling
- Document required environment variables

---

## Integration with Claude Code

### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code

1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
2. **Use task template** for all planning
3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
4. **Fill review section** when done
5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged

### Behavioral Guidelines

**Before starting ANY task:**
- Read `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
- Create task file from template
- Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)

**During implementation:**
- Update todos as you complete them
- Document decisions in task file
- Follow simplicity principle

**When complete:**
- Fill out review section
- Update docs/ if architecture changed
- Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)

**On every commit:**
- Stage code files AND task file together
- Git hook verifies compliance
- Commit proceeds

### Using Skills

Available skills can be invoked explicitly:
```
Use [skill-name] to [action]
```

---

## Appendix

### Git Hook Exit Codes

- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)

### ANSI Color Codes Used

```bash
RED='\033[0;31m'      # Errors, blocking messages
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'   # Warnings, instructions
GREEN='\033[0;32m'    # Success messages
BLUE='\033[0;34m'     # Informational content
NC='\033[0m'          # Reset (no color)
```

---

## See Also

- `CLAUDE.md` - Complete workflow rules
- `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Step-by-step checklist
- `.claude/templates/task-template.md` - Task file template
- `.claude/skills/README.md` - Available skills
- `.claude/commands/` - Custom commands

**Remember:** These tools exist to help, not hinder. Follow the workflow for smoother, more consistent development across ANY project.

   130→━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
   131→
   132→Code files were changed, but no task file is staged.
   133→
   134→Changed code files:
   135→  - src/components/Modal.tsx
   136→  - src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
   137→
   138→Required Action:
   139→  1. Update your task file (tasks/[feature-name].md)
   140→  2. Stage your task file: git add tasks/[feature-name].md
   141→  3. Retry commit: git commit
   142→```
   143→
   144→**Emergency Bypass:**
   145→```bash
   146→git commit --no-verify
   147→```
   148→**Use only for:** Hotfixes, doc-only changes, reverts, emergencies

   242→## Setup Instructions
   243→
   244→### File Structure (Generic)
   245→
   246→```
   247→[project-root]/
   248→├── .git/
   249→│   └── hooks/
   250→│       └── pre-commit              # Git hook (enforces compliance)
   251→├── .claude/
   252→│   ├── automation-guide.md         (this file)
   253→│   ├── workflow-checklist.md       (step-by-step checklist)
   254→│   ├── settings.json               (Claude settings)
   255→│   ├── settings.local.json         (local overrides)
   256→│   ├── templates/
   257→│   │   └── task-template.md        (task file template)
   258→│   ├── commands/
   259→│   │   └── start-task.md           (custom commands)
   260→│   └── skills/
   261→│       └── [skill-name]/           (custom skills)
   262→├── tasks/                           (task files go here)
   263→├── CLAUDE.md                        (main workflow guide)
   264→└── README.md                        (project documentation)
   265→```
   266→
   267→### Initial Setup (One-Time)
   268→
   269→**Verify setup:**
   270→```bash
   271→# Check git hook exists and is executable
   272→ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   273→
   274→# Check automation files exist
   275→ls -la .claude/
   276→
   277→# Make hook executable (if needed)
   278→chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
   279→```
   280→
   281→### For New Developers/Contributors
   282→
   283→1. **Clone repository**
   284→2. **Read CLAUDE.md** (workflow rules)
   285→3. **Read this guide** (you're here!)
   286→4. **Read workflow checklist:** `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   287→5. **Test git hook:**
   288→   ```bash
   289→   # Make a small test change
   290→   echo "// test" >> src/main.tsx  # or index.js, etc.
   291→
   292→   # Try to commit without staging task file
   293→   git add src/main.tsx
   294→   git commit -m "test"
   295→   # Should be BLOCKED
   296→
   297→   # Stage task file and retry
   298→   git add tasks/some-task.md
   299→   git commit -m "test"
   300→   # Should succeed
   301→
   302→   # Revert test
   303→   git reset HEAD~1
   304→   git checkout src/main.tsx
   305→   ```

   309→## How It Works
   310→
   311→### Git Hook Workflow
   312→
   313→```
   314→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   315→│  Developer runs: git commit                                  │
   316→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   317→                     │
   318→                     ▼
   319→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   320→│  Pre-commit hook executes (.git/hooks/pre-commit)           │
   321→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   322→                     │
   323→                     ▼
   324→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   325→│  Check: Are any code files staged?                          │
   326→│  (src/, components/, widgets/, stores/, services/, lib/)    │
   327→└────────────┬────────────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
   328→             │ YES                    │ NO
   329→             ▼                        ▼
   330→┌──────────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
   331→│ Check: Is tasks/*.md     │  │ Allow commit                 │
   332→│ file staged?             │  │ (doc-only, config, etc.)     │
   333→└──────┬───────────┬───────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘
   334→       │ YES       │ NO
   335→       ▼           ▼
   336→┌────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   337→│ Allow      │  │ BLOCK commit                                │
   338→│ commit     │  │ Display error message with instructions     │
   339→└────────────┘  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   340→```
   341→
   342→### Task File Lifecycle
   343→
   344→```
   345→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   346→│  1. Start Task                                               │
   347→│     - Copy task-template.md to tasks/[feature].md           │
   348→│     - Fill out overview, problem, files to read             │
   349→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   350→                     │
   351→                     ▼
   352→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   353→│  2. Plan Phase                                               │
   354→│     - Add proposed changes                                   │
   355→│     - Create todo checklist                                  │
   356→│     - Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)                      │
   357→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   358→                     │
   359→                     ▼
   360→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   361→│  3. Implementation                                           │
   362→│     - Mark todos as in_progress/completed                   │
   363→│     - Add implementation notes                               │
   364→│     - Document decisions and challenges                      │
   365→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   366→                     │
   367→                     ▼
   368→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   369→│  4. Completion                                               │
   370→│     - Fill out review section                                │
   371→│     - Document all files created/modified                    │
   372→│     - Add testing status                                     │
   373→│     - Update docs/ if architecture changed                   │
   374→└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
   375→                     │
   376→                     ▼
   377→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   378→│  5. Commit                                                   │
   379→│     - Stage all files: git add .                            │
   380→│     - Write descriptive commit message                       │
   381→│     - Push to remote: git push origin [branch]              │
   382→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   383→```
   384→
   385→---
   386→
   387→## Troubleshooting
   388→
   389→### Issue 1: Pre-Commit Hook Not Running
   390→
   391→**Symptoms:**
   392→- Can commit code without task file
   393→- No error message appears
   394→
   395→**Causes & Fixes:**
   396→
   397→**Cause 1:** Hook not executable
   398→```bash
   399→# Fix: Make it executable
   400→chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
   401→```
   402→
   403→**Cause 2:** Hook file doesn't exist
   404→```bash
   405→# Check if file exists
   406→ls -la .git/hooks/pre-commit
   407→
   408→# If missing, recreate from template
   409→```
   410→
   411→**Cause 3:** Using `--no-verify` flag
   412→```bash
   413→# Don't use --no-verify unless emergency
   414→git commit  # Correct
   415→git commit --no-verify  # Bypasses hook
   416→```
   417→
   418→**Cause 4:** Windows Git Bash issues
   419→```bash
   420→# Ensure Git Bash is being used (not cmd.exe or PowerShell for hooks)
   421→# Check shebang in hook file: #!/bin/bash
   422→# Note: On Windows, you may need to use Git Bash terminal
   423→```
   424→
   425→---
   426→
   427→### Issue 2: Hook Blocking Valid Commits
   428→
   429→**Symptoms:**
   430→- Hook blocks commit even though task file is staged
   431→- False positive error
   432→
   433→**Causes & Fixes:**
   434→
   435→**Cause 1:** Task file in wrong location
   436→```bash
   437→# Task files MUST be in tasks/ directory
   438→git add tasks/my-feature.md  # Correct
   439→git add my-feature.md         # Wrong location
   440→git add docs/task.md          # Wrong location
   441→```
   442→
   443→**Cause 2:** Task file not staged
   444→```bash
   445→# Check what's staged
   446→git diff --cached --name-only
   447→
   448→# Ensure task file is in the list
   449→# If not, stage it:
   450→git add tasks/my-feature.md
   451→```
   452→
   453→**Cause 3:** Wrong file extension
   454→```bash
   455→# Task files MUST be .md (markdown)
   456→git add tasks/my-feature.md   # Correct
   457→git add tasks/my-feature.txt  # Wrong extension
   458→```
   459→
   460→---
   461→
   462→### Issue 3: Can't Find Task Template
   463→
   464→**Symptoms:**
   465→- `cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/...` fails
   466→- File not found error
   467→
   468→**Fixes:**
   469→```bash
   470→# Check if template exists
   471→ls -la .claude/templates/task-template.md
   472→
   473→# If missing, check .claude/ directory structure
   474→ls -la .claude/
   475→
   476→# Ensure you're in repository root
   477→pwd
   478→```
   479→
   480→---
   481→
   482→### Issue 4: Hook Allows Code Without Task File
   483→
   484→**Symptoms:**
   485→- Code commits go through without task file
   486→- Hook seems to not detect code changes
   487→
   488→**Causes & Fixes:**
   489→
   490→**Cause 1:** Code not in monitored directories
   491→```bash
   492→# Hook checks specific directories (src/, lib/, components/, etc.)
   493→# If code elsewhere, hook won't detect it
   494→
   495→# Solution: Update hook to check additional directories
   496→# Edit .git/hooks/pre-commit
   497→# Find the grep pattern line and add more directories
   498→```
   499→
   500→**Cause 2:** Only .md files changed in src/
   501→```bash
   502→# Hook ignores .md files in src/
   503→# (to allow README updates without task file)
   504→# This is intentional behavior
   505→```
   506→
   507→---
   508→
   509→### Issue 5: Hook Error Messages Not Displaying
   510→
   511→**Symptoms:**
   512→- Commit blocked but no error message shown
   513→- Silent failure
   514→
   515→**Fixes:**
   516→```bash
   517→# Test hook manually
   518→bash .git/hooks/pre-commit
   519→
   520→# Check for syntax errors
   521→bash -n .git/hooks/pre-commit
   522→
   523→# Ensure ANSI colors work in terminal
   524→# Some terminals don't support color codes
   525→```
   526→
   527→---
   528→
   529→## Customization
   530→
   531→### Adding More File Patterns to Hook
   532→
   533→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
   534→
   535→```bash
   536→# Current pattern (example)
   537→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v '\.md$')
   538→
   539→# Add more directories
   540→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^(src/|lib/|config/|components/|services/)' | grep -v '\.md$')
   541→```
   542→
   543→### Excluding Specific Files from Hook
   544→
   545→**Edit:** `.git/hooks/pre-commit`
   546→
   547→```bash
   548→# Exclude specific files (e.g., generated files)
   549→CODE_FILES_CHANGED=$(echo "$STAGED_FILES" | grep -E '^src/' | grep -v -E '(\.md$|generated\.ts$|\.d\.ts$)')
   550→```
   551→
   552→### Customizing Task Template
   553→
   554→**Edit:** `.claude/templates/task-template.md`
   555→
   556→**Example additions:**

   558→## Security Checklist
   559→- [ ] Input validation added
   560→- [ ] XSS prevention verified
   561→- [ ] SQL injection prevented
   562→- [ ] CSRF protection verified
   563→
   564→## Performance Checklist
   565→- [ ] Bundle size impact acceptable
   566→- [ ] No memory leaks
   567→- [ ] Animations 60fps
   568→- [ ] Lazy loading where appropriate
   569→
   570→## Accessibility Checklist
   571→- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
   572→- [ ] Screen reader compatible
   573→- [ ] Color contrast sufficient
   574→- [ ] Focus indicators visible
   575→
   576→## Browser/Device Testing
   577→- [ ] Chrome
   578→- [ ] Firefox
   579→- [ ] Safari
   580→- [ ] Edge
   581→- [ ] Mobile (375px)
   582→- [ ] Tablet (768px)
   583→- [ ] Desktop (1024px+)
   584→```
   585→
   586→### Adding New Custom Commands
   587→
   588→**Create:** `.claude/commands/[command-name].md`
   589→
   590→Example: `.claude/commands/review-task.md`
   591→```markdown
   592→---
   593→description: Review task before marking complete
   594→---
   595→
   596→# Review Task Checklist
   597→
   598→Before marking task complete:
   599→
   600→- [ ] Review section filled out
   601→- [ ] All files documented

   606→
   607→### Adding More Skills
   608→
   609→1. Create folder: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/`
   610→2. Create file: `.claude/skills/[skill-name]/SKILL.md`
   611→3. Follow existing skill format (YAML frontmatter + content)

   615→
   616→## Best Practices
   617→
   618→### 1. Always Start with the Checklist
   619→
   620→```bash
   621→cat .claude/workflow-checklist.md
   622→# Or use custom command
   623→/start-task
   624→```
   625→
   626→### 2. Create Task File FIRST
   627→
   628→```bash
   629→# CORRECT ORDER
   630→cp .claude/templates/task-template.md tasks/my-feature.md
   631→# Fill out the task file
   632→# THEN start coding
   633→
   634→# WRONG ORDER
   635→# Start coding first
   636→# Create task file later (will forget details!)
   637→```
   638→
   639→### 3. Update Task File as You Work
   640→
   641→```markdown
   642→## Todo Checklist
   643→
   644→### Phase 1
   645→- [x] ~~Task 1~~ - DONE
   646→- [ ] Task 2 - IN PROGRESS
   647→- [ ] Task 3 - Blocked (waiting for input)
   648→```
   649→
   650→### 4. Fill Review Section Immediately
   651→
   652→When work is complete, fill out the review section RIGHT AWAY:
   653→- Summary of changes
   654→- Files created/modified
   655→- Testing status
   656→- Issues encountered
   657→
   658→Don't wait - you'll forget important details!
   659→
   660→### 5. Stage Task File with Code
   661→
   662→```bash
   663→# CORRECT - Stage both together
   664→git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   665→git add tasks/add-modal-system.md
   666→git commit -m "feat: Add modal system"
   667→
   668→# WRONG - Separate commits (hook will block)
   669→git add src/components/Modal.tsx
   670→git commit -m "feat: Add modal"
   671→```
   672→
   673→### 6. Use Meaningful Task File Names
   674→
   675→```bash
   676→# GOOD - Descriptive, specific
   677→tasks/add-undo-system-for-notes.md
   678→tasks/fix-calendar-timezone-bug.md
   679→tasks/refactor-state-stores.md
   680→
   681→# BAD - Vague, generic
   682→tasks/todo.md
   683→tasks/task1.md
   684→tasks/stuff.md
   685→```
   686→
   687→### 7. One Task File Per Feature
   688→
   689→Don't reuse task files for multiple unrelated features.
   690→
   691→### 8. Emergency Bypass is for EMERGENCIES
   692→
   693→```bash
   694→# Use --no-verify ONLY for:
   695→git commit --no-verify  # Hotfix (site down)
   696→git commit --no-verify  # Revert broken commit
   697→git commit --no-verify  # Critical security fix
   698→
   699→# NOT for:
   700→git commit --no-verify  # "I'm lazy"
   701→git commit --no-verify  # "I'll update task file later"
   702→```
   703→
   704→### 9. Keep Documentation Updated
   705→
   706→If architecture changes, update docs:
   707→```bash
   708→git add src/stores/useUndoStore.ts
   709→git add tasks/add-undo-system.md
   710→git add docs/technical/undo_system_architecture.md
   711→git commit -m "feat: Add undo system"
   712→```
   713→
   714→### 10. Screenshot Before UI Changes
   715→
   716→For ANY visual/UI change:
   717→1. Screenshot current state
   718→2. Make ONE change (not multiple at once)
   719→3. Screenshot after
   720→4. Compare to baseline
   721→5. Get user approval before committing
   722→
   723→### 11. Test Automation with Dry Run
   724→
   725→```bash
   726→# CORRECT
   727→DRY_RUN=true python automation/script.py
   728→# Verify output looks good
   729→# THEN remove DRY_RUN for real execution
   730→
   731→# WRONG
   732→# Just run live and hope it works
   733→```
   734→
   735→### 12. Meaningful Commit Messages
   736→
   737→```bash
   738→# GOOD
   739→git commit -m "feat: Add YouTube social link
   740→
   741→- Added new link to social menu
   742→- Updated animation timing
   743→- Tested on all browsers
   744→
   745→Generated with Claude Code
   746→Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>"
   747→
   748→# BAD
   749→git commit -m "update stuff"
   750→```
   751→

   753→
   754→## Project-Specific Configurations
   755→
   756→### Static Site Projects (HTML/CSS/JS)
   757→
   758→- No build tools required
   759→- Use `python3 -m http.server 8000` for local testing
   760→- Focus on browser compatibility testing
   761→- Consider: GIF optimization, lazy loading
   762→
   763→### React/Vue/Angular Projects
   764→
   765→- Run `npm run test` before commits
   766→- Run `npm run type-check` for TypeScript
   767→- Run `npm run lint` for code style
   768→- Run `npm run build` to verify bundle size
   769→- Test in light mode AND dark mode
   770→
   771→### Full-Stack Projects
   772→
   773→- Test both frontend and backend changes
   774→- Verify API contracts unchanged (or documented)
   775→- Check database migrations
   776→- Test authentication flows
   777→
   778→### Automation Projects
   779→
   780→- Always use DRY_RUN=true first
   781→- Verify API keys are properly stored
   782→- Test error handling
   783→- Document required environment variables
   784→

   787→## Integration with Claude Code
   788→
   789→### Using Automation Tools with Claude Code
   790→
   791→1. **Reference workflow checklist** at start of session
   792→2. **Use task template** for all planning
   793→3. **Update task file** as you work (mark todos complete)
   794→4. **Fill review section** when done
   795→5. **Git hook verifies** task file is staged
   796→
   797→### Behavioral Guidelines
   798→
   799→**Before starting ANY task:**
   800→- Read `.claude/workflow-checklist.md`
   801→- Create task file from template
   802→- Get user approval (ExitPlanMode)
   803→
   804→**During implementation:**
   805→- Update todos as you complete them
   806→- Document decisions in task file
   807→- Follow simplicity principle
   808→
   809→**When complete:**
   810→- Fill out review section
   811→- Update docs/ if architecture changed
   812→- Verify git hook will pass (task file staged)
   813→
   814→**On every commit:**
   815→- Stage code files AND task file together
   816→- Git hook verifies compliance
   817→- Commit proceeds
   818→
   819→### Using Skills
   820→
   821→Available skills can be invoked explicitly:
   822→```
   823→Use [skill-name] to [action]
   824→```

   828→## Appendix
   829→
   830→### Git Hook Exit Codes
   831→
   832→- `exit 0` - Success (commit allowed)
   833→- `exit 1` - Failure (commit blocked)
   834→
