1→# Claude Code Resources - Comprehensive Research Summary
     2→
     3→**Compiled:** December 2025
     4→**Purpose:** Reference guide for optimizing Claude Code utilization
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Table of Contents
     9→
    10→1. [Awesome Lists & Plugin Collections](#awesome-lists--plugin-collections)
    11→2. [Frameworks](#frameworks)
    12→3. [Subagents Collections](#subagents-collections)
    13→4. [Hooks & Automation](#hooks--automation)
    14→5. [Slash Commands](#slash-commands)
    15→6. [CLAUDE.md Best Practices](#claudemd-best-practices)
    16→7. [Status Line Tools](#status-line-tools)
    17→8. [Output Styles](#output-styles)
    18→9. [MCP Servers](#mcp-servers)
    19→10. [IDE Integrations](#ide-integrations)
    20→11. [Tips & Tricks](#tips--tricks)
    21→12. [Key Resources](#key-resources)
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## Awesome Lists & Plugin Collections
    26→
    27→### Primary Collections
    28→
    29→| Repository | Stars | Description |
    30→|------------|-------|-------------|
    31→| [ccplugins/awesome-claude-code-plugins](https://github.com/ccplugins/awesome-claude-code-plugins) | - | Curated list of slash commands, subagents, MCP servers, hooks |
    32→| [jqueryscript/awesome-claude-code](https://github.com/jqueryscript/awesome-claude-code) | - | Tools, IDE integrations, frameworks, 100+ subagents |
    33→| [jeremylongshore/claude-code-plugins-plus](https://github.com/jeremylongshore/claude-code-plugins-plus) | - | 243 plugins, 175 with Agent Skills v1.2.0 |
    34→| [hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code](https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code) | - | Hooks, slash commands, subagent files, workflows |
    35→| [quemsah/awesome-claude-plugins](https://github.com/quemsah/awesome-claude-plugins) | - | 1,269 indexed repositories, automated metrics |
    36→| [alvinunreal/awesome-claude](https://github.com/alvinunreal/awesome-claude) | - | General Claude resources |
    37→
    38→### Official Plugin System (October 2025)
    39→- Install plugins with `/plugin` command
    40→- Works across terminal and VS Code
    41→- Bundle slash commands, subagents, MCP servers, and hooks
    42→
    43→---
    44→
    45→## Frameworks
    46→
    47→### SuperClaude Framework
    48→
    49→**Repository:** [SuperClaude-Org/SuperClaude_Framework](https://github.com/SuperClaude-Org/SuperClaude_Framework)
    50→**Website:** [superclaude.org](https://superclaude.org/)
    51→
    52→**Features:**
    53→- 25 specialized commands (`/sc:*` prefix)
    54→- 9 Cognitive Personas with automatic activation
    55→- Behavioral instruction injection
    56→- Component orchestration
    57→
    58→**Cognitive Personas:**
    59→| Persona | Domain | Activation Triggers |
    60→|---------|--------|---------------------|
    61→| architect | System design | Architecture discussions |
    62→| frontend | UI/UX | .tsx files, styling |
    63→| backend | API/Database | Server code, APIs |
    64→| security | Security audit | Security keywords |
    65→| analyzer | Debugging | "bug", "error" keywords |
    66→| qa | Testing | Test discussions |
    67→| performance | Optimization | Performance issues |
    68→| refactorer | Code cleanup | Refactoring tasks |
    69→| mentor | Teaching | Explanations needed |
    70→
    71→**Installation:**
    72→```bash
    73→pipx install superclaude
    74→superclaude install
    75→```
    76→
    77→**Example Commands:**
    78→```bash
    79→/design --api --ddd --architect
    80→/build --feature --tdd --backend
    81→/test --coverage --e2e
    82→/analyze
    83→/troubleshoot
    84→```
    85→
    86→### Other Orchestrators
    87→
    88→| Tool | Description |
    89→|------|-------------|
    90→| claude-flow | Multi-agent orchestration |
    91→| claude-squad | Team coordination |
    92→| ccpm | Claude Code Package Manager |
    93→
    94→---
    95→
    96→## Subagents Collections
    97→
    98→### Official Structure
    99→
   100→Location: `.claude/agents/` (project) or `~/.claude/agents/` (global)
   101→
   102→**Configuration Format:**
   103→```yaml
   104→---
   105→name: agent-name
   106→description: When to use this agent
   107→tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep]
   108→model: sonnet  # or opus, haiku, inherit
   109→permissionMode: default
   110→skills: [optional-skills]
   111→---
   112→
   113→System prompt content here...
   114→```
   115→
   116→**Model Options:**
   117→- `sonnet` - Default, balanced
   118→- `opus` - Most capable
   119→- `haiku` - Fastest, cheapest
   120→- `inherit` - Use parent model
   121→
   122→### Popular Collections
   123→
   124→| Repository | Agents | Focus |
   125→|------------|--------|-------|
   126→| [VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents](https://github.com/VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents) | 100+ | Full-stack, DevOps, data science |
   127→| [wshobson/agents](https://github.com/wshobson/agents) | 91 | 15 orchestrators, 47 skills, 45 tools |
   128→| [0xfurai/claude-code-subagents](https://github.com/0xfurai/claude-code-subagents) | 100+ | Production-ready, domain experts |
   129→| [lst97/claude-code-sub-agents](https://github.com/lst97/claude-code-sub-agents) | - | Full-stack development |
   130→| [zhsama/claude-sub-agent](https://github.com/zhsama/claude-sub-agent) | - | Phased workflow system |
   131→| [vanzan01/claude-code-sub-agent-collective](https://github.com/vanzan01/claude-code-sub-agent-collective) | - | Context engineering, hub-spoke coordination |
   132→
   133→### Tool Assignment Guidelines
   134→
   135→| Agent Type | Recommended Tools |
   136→|------------|-------------------|
   137→| Read-only/Research | Read, Grep, Glob |
   138→| Web Research | WebFetch, WebSearch |
   139→| Code Writers | Read, Write, Edit, Bash |
   140→| Full Access | All tools |
   141→
   142→---
   143→
   144→## Hooks & Automation
   145→
   146→### Hook Types
   147→
   148→| Hook | Trigger | Use Cases |
   149→|------|---------|-----------|
   150→| PreToolUse | Before any tool executes | Validation, blocking |
   151→| PostToolUse | After tool completes | Logging, formatting |
   152→| Stop | Session ends | Cleanup, notifications |
   153→| SessionStart | Session begins | Environment setup |
   154→| UserPromptSubmit | User submits prompt | Input validation |
   155→
   156→### Configuration Locations
   157→
   158→- **Global:** `~/.claude/settings.json`
   159→- **Project:** `.claude/settings.json`
   160→
   161→### Example: Pre-Commit Linting
   162→
   163→```json
   164→{
   165→  "hooks": {
   166→    "PreToolUse": [
   167→      {
   168→        "matcher": "Bash(git commit:*)",
   169→        "command": "~/.claude/hooks/pre-commit.sh"
   170→      }
   171→    ]
   172→  }
   173→}
   174→```
   175→
   176→### Common Hook Use Cases
   177→
   178→**Pre-Commit:**
   179→- Run linters (ESLint, Prettier, Rubocop)
   180→- Run tests before commit
   181→- Secret scanning
   182→- TODO/FIXME detection
   183→- Automatic ticket ID association
   184→- Co-author trailer addition
   185→
   186→**Post-Commit:**
   187→- Notifications
   188→- Sync with external services
   189→- Logging
   190→
   191→**Blocking Protected Files:**
   192→```python
   193→# Return exit code 2 to block
   194→if file_path in ['.env', 'package-lock.json']:
   195→    sys.exit(2)
   196→```
   197→
   198→### Headless Mode for CI
   199→
   200→```bash
   201→claude -p "Your prompt" --output-format stream-json
   202→```
   203→
   204→---
   205→
   206→## Slash Commands
   207→
   208→### Built-in Commands
   209→
   210→| Command | Purpose |
   211→|---------|---------|
   212→| `/init` | Generate CLAUDE.md from project analysis |
   213→| `/clear` | Clear context, start fresh |
   214→| `/compact` | Summarize conversation (auto at 80%) |
   215→| `/memory` | Manage CLAUDE.md content |
   216→| `/output-style` | Change response formatting |
   217→| `/statusline` | Configure status line |
   218→| `/terminal-setup` | Fix terminal keybindings |
   219→| `/plugin` | Browse/install plugins |
   220→| `/install-github-app` | Enable PR auto-review |
   221→
   222→### Custom Commands
   223→
   224→**Location:** `.claude/commands/` (project) or `~/.claude/commands/` (global)
   225→
   226→**Format:** Markdown files that become slash commands
   227→
   228→```markdown
   229→<!-- .claude/commands/debug-loop.md -->
   230→# Debug Loop
   231→
   232→Analyze the error, propose fixes, and test until resolved.
   233→Focus on: $ARGUMENTS
   234→```
   235→
   236→**Usage:** `/debug-loop TypeError in auth module`
   237→
   238→### Popular Command Collections
   239→
   240→- [awattar/claude-code-best-practices](https://github.com/awattar/claude-code-best-practices) - `/custom-init`, `/help-commands`
   241→- SuperClaude - 25 specialized commands
   242→
   243→---
   244→
   245→## CLAUDE.md Best Practices
   246→
   247→### File Locations
   248→
   249→| Location | Scope | Use For |
   250→|----------|-------|---------|
   251→| `./CLAUDE.md` | Project | Architecture, standards, deployment |
   252→| `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Global | Personal preferences, workflows |
   253→
   254→### Key Principles
   255→
   256→1. **Initialize with `/init`** - Generates from project analysis
   257→2. **Iterate and refine** - Like any frequently used prompt
   258→3. **Keep concise** - Human-readable, no fluff
   259→4. **Use `#` key** - Add instructions you repeat often
   260→5. **Share via git** - Project CLAUDE.md benefits whole team
   261→
   262→### Recommended Content
   263→
   264→```markdown
   265→# Project Name
   266→
   267→## Architecture
   268→- Key patterns and decisions
   269→- Folder structure explanation
   270→
   271→## Coding Standards
   272→- Style guide references
   273→- Naming conventions
   274→
   275→## Common Commands
   276→- Build: `npm run build`
   277→- Test: `npm test`
   278→- Deploy: `./deploy.sh`
   279→
   280→## Important Files
   281→- Entry point: src/index.ts
   282→- Config: config/settings.ts
   283→
   284→## Things That Work
   285→- Pattern X for problem Y
   286→
   287→## Things That Don't Work
   288→- Avoid pattern Z because...
   289→```
   290→
   291→### Performance Impact
   292→
   293→- Repository-specific optimization: **+11% better code**
   294→- System prompt optimization: **+5% general performance**
   295→
   296→### Key Resources
   297→
   298→- [Cranot/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/Cranot/claude-code-guide)
   299→- [zebbern/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/zebbern/claude-code-guide)
   300→- [awattar/claude-code-best-practices](https://github.com/awattar/claude-code-best-practices)
   301→- [Official Blog](https://claude.com/blog/using-claude-md-files)
   302→
   303→---
   304→
   305→## Status Line Tools
   306→
   307→### Official Configuration
   308→
   309→**Location:** `.claude/settings.json`
   310→
   311→```json
   312→{
   313→  "statusLine": {
   314→    "type": "command",
   315→    "command": "~/.claude/statusline.sh",
   316→    "padding": 0
   317→  }
   318→}
   319→```
   320→
   321→Claude passes session JSON via stdin (model, directories, git info).
   322→
   323→### Popular Tools
   324→
   325→| Tool | Stars | Features |
   326→|------|-------|----------|
   327→| [sirmalloc/ccstatusline](https://github.com/sirmalloc/ccstatusline) | 2.3k | Multi-line, TUI config, themes, powerline |
   328→| [rz1989s/claude-code-statusline](https://github.com/rz1989s/claude-code-statusline) | - | Cost tracking, MCP monitoring, themes |
   329→| [chongdashu/cc-statusline](https://github.com/chongdashu/cc-statusline) | - | Bash-first, zero dependencies |
   330→| [gabriel-dehan/claude_monitor_statusline](https://github.com/gabriel-dehan/claude_monitor_statusline) | - | Usage info display |
   331→
   332→### Installation (ccstatusline)
   333→
   334→```bash
   335→npx ccstatusline@latest
   336→# or
   337→bunx ccstatusline@latest
   338→```
   339→
   340→### Key Metrics to Display
   341→
   342→- **Context usage %** - Most important (stop around 60%)
   343→- Model name
   344→- Current directory
   345→- Git branch
   346→- Uncommitted file count
   347→- Token usage progress bar
   348→- Cost tracking
   349→
   350→**Why Monitor Context:**
   351→- 200K token window
   352→- Auto-compacts at 80%
   353→- Performance degrades as context fills
   354→
   355→---
   356→
   357→## Output Styles
   358→
   359→### Configuration
   360→
   361→```json
   362→{
   363→  "outputStyle": "default"
   364→}
   365→```
   366→
   367→### Custom Styles
   368→
   369→**Locations:**
   370→- `~/.claude/output-styles/` (global)
   371→- `.claude/output-styles/` (project)
   372→
   373→**Selection:** `/output-style` command
   374→
   375→**Persistence:** Stored in `.claude/settings.local.json`
   376→
   377→### Verbose Mode
   378→
   379→```bash
   380→claude --verbose  # Detailed logging for debugging
   381→```
   382→
   383→---
   384→
   385→## MCP Servers
   386→
   387→### Recommended Servers
   388→
   389→| Server | Package | Purpose |
   390→|--------|---------|---------|
   391→| sequential-thinking | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking` | Complex reasoning |
   392→| playwright | `@playwright/mcp@latest` | Browser automation |
   393→| filesystem | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem` | File operations |
   394→| sqlite | `uvx mcp-server-sqlite` | Database queries |
   395→| memory | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-memory` | Persistent memory |
   396→| fetch | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-fetch` | Web fetching |
   397→| puppeteer | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-puppeteer` | Browser control |
   398→| brave-search | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search` | Web search |
   399→
   400→### Configuration
   401→
   402→**Location:** `~/.claude.json`
   403→
   404→```json
   405→{
   406→  "mcpServers": {
   407→    "sequential-thinking": {
   408→      "command": "npx",
   409→      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"]
   410→    }
   411→  }
   412→}
   413→```
   414→
   415→### Lazy Loading
   416→
   417→Set `"enableAllProjectMcpServers": false` in settings to only load MCP servers when explicitly called.
   418→
   419→---
   420→
   421→## IDE Integrations
   422→
   423→| IDE | Extension | Features |
   424→|-----|-----------|----------|
   425→| VS Code | Official Claude Code | Inline diffs, real-time edits |
   426→| VS Code | Claude Code Chat | Native chat interface, history |
   427→| Neovim | claudecode.nvim | Pure Lua integration |
   428→| Cursor | Claude-Autopilot | Task automation |
   429→| GitButler | Hooks integration | Branch management |
   430→
   431→---
   432→
   433→## Tips & Tricks
   434→
   435→### Permission Management
   436→
   437→```bash
   438→# Skip all permission prompts (use carefully)
   439→claude --dangerously-skip-permissions
   440→
   441→# Pre-approve common commands
   442→claude allow "git commit"
   443→claude allow "npm install"
   444→```
   445→
   446→### Keyboard Shortcuts
   447→
   448→| Action | Key |
   449→|--------|-----|
   450→| Stop Claude | `Escape` |
   451→| Previous messages | `Escape` twice |
   452→| Paste images | `Ctrl+V` (not Cmd+V) |
   453→| New line | Run `/terminal-setup` first |
   454→
   455→### Thinking Depth
   456→
   457→| Phrase | Depth |
   458→|--------|-------|
   459→| "think" | Standard analysis |
   460→| "think hard" | Deeper analysis |
   461→| "ultrathink" | Maximum depth |
   462→
   463→### Context Management
   464→
   465→- **Start fresh:** `/clear` regularly
   466→- **Auto-compact:** Happens at 80% context
   467→- **Monitor:** Use status line for context %
   468→- **Subagents:** Use for complex tasks to preserve main context
   469→
   470→### Parallel Sessions
   471→
   472→```bash
   473→# Use git worktree for parallel branches
   474→git worktree add ../feature-branch feature-branch
   475→cd ../feature-branch
   476→claude  # Separate session
   477→```
   478→
   479→### Best Practices
   480→
   481→1. **Read before modifying** - Never propose changes to unread code
   482→2. **Use subagents early** - Preserves main context
   483→3. **Clear prompts** - Use XML-like tags for structure
   484→4. **Structured prompting** - Specify exact tasks, files, outputs
   485→5. **Fresh sessions** - Reduce prompt-drift
   486→
   487→---
   488→
   489→## Key Resources
   490→
   491→### Official Documentation
   492→- [Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs)
   493→- [Best Practices](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices)
   494→- [CLAUDE.md Guide](https://claude.com/blog/using-claude-md-files)
   495→- [Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)
   496→
   497→### Community Guides
   498→- [ClaudeLog](https://claudelog.com/) - Docs, tutorials, best practices
   499→- [awesomeclaude.ai](https://awesomeclaude.ai/) - Comprehensive resource hub
   500→- [32 Claude Code Tips](https://agenticcoding.substack.com/p/32-claude-code-tips-from-basics-to)
   501→- [Cooking with Claude Code](https://www.siddharthbharath.com/claude-code-the-complete-guide/)
   502→
   503→### GitHub Guides
   504→- [Cranot/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/Cranot/claude-code-guide)
   505→- [zebbern/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/zebbern/claude-code-guide)
   506→- [wesammustafa/Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know](https://github.com/wesammustafa/Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know)
   507→
   508→### Blog Posts
   509→- [Demystifying Claude Code Hooks](https://www.brethorsting.com/blog/2025/08/demystifying-claude-code-hooks/)
   510→- [Hooks for Automated Quality Checks](https://www.letanure.dev/blog/2025-08-06--claude-code-part-8-hooks-automated-quality-checks)
   511→- [Optimizing CLAUDE.md](https://arize.com/blog/claude-md-best-practices-learned-from-optimizing-claude-code-with-prompt-learning/)
   512→
   513→---
   514→
   515→## Quick Reference
   516→
   517→### File Locations Summary
   518→
   519→| Purpose | Project Location | Global Location |
   520→|---------|------------------|-----------------|

file.content:
# Claude Code Resources - Comprehensive Research Summary

**Compiled:** December 2025
**Purpose:** Reference guide for optimizing Claude Code utilization

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Awesome Lists & Plugin Collections](#awesome-lists--plugin-collections)
2. [Frameworks](#frameworks)
3. [Subagents Collections](#subagents-collections)
4. [Hooks & Automation](#hooks--automation)
5. [Slash Commands](#slash-commands)
6. [CLAUDE.md Best Practices](#claudemd-best-practices)
7. [Status Line Tools](#status-line-tools)
8. [Output Styles](#output-styles)
9. [MCP Servers](#mcp-servers)
10. [IDE Integrations](#ide-integrations)
11. [Tips & Tricks](#tips--tricks)
12. [Key Resources](#key-resources)

---

## Awesome Lists & Plugin Collections

### Primary Collections

| Repository | Stars | Description |
|------------|-------|-------------|
| [ccplugins/awesome-claude-code-plugins](https://github.com/ccplugins/awesome-claude-code-plugins) | - | Curated list of slash commands, subagents, MCP servers, hooks |
| [jqueryscript/awesome-claude-code](https://github.com/jqueryscript/awesome-claude-code) | - | Tools, IDE integrations, frameworks, 100+ subagents |
| [jeremylongshore/claude-code-plugins-plus](https://github.com/jeremylongshore/claude-code-plugins-plus) | - | 243 plugins, 175 with Agent Skills v1.2.0 |
| [hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code](https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code) | - | Hooks, slash commands, subagent files, workflows |
| [quemsah/awesome-claude-plugins](https://github.com/quemsah/awesome-claude-plugins) | - | 1,269 indexed repositories, automated metrics |
| [alvinunreal/awesome-claude](https://github.com/alvinunreal/awesome-claude) | - | General Claude resources |

### Official Plugin System (October 2025)
- Install plugins with `/plugin` command
- Works across terminal and VS Code
- Bundle slash commands, subagents, MCP servers, and hooks

---

## Frameworks

### SuperClaude Framework

**Repository:** [SuperClaude-Org/SuperClaude_Framework](https://github.com/SuperClaude-Org/SuperClaude_Framework)
**Website:** [superclaude.org](https://superclaude.org/)

**Features:**
- 25 specialized commands (`/sc:*` prefix)
- 9 Cognitive Personas with automatic activation
- Behavioral instruction injection
- Component orchestration

**Cognitive Personas:**
| Persona | Domain | Activation Triggers |
|---------|--------|---------------------|
| architect | System design | Architecture discussions |
| frontend | UI/UX | .tsx files, styling |
| backend | API/Database | Server code, APIs |
| security | Security audit | Security keywords |
| analyzer | Debugging | "bug", "error" keywords |
| qa | Testing | Test discussions |
| performance | Optimization | Performance issues |
| refactorer | Code cleanup | Refactoring tasks |
| mentor | Teaching | Explanations needed |

**Installation:**
```bash
pipx install superclaude
superclaude install
```

**Example Commands:**
```bash
/design --api --ddd --architect
/build --feature --tdd --backend
/test --coverage --e2e
/analyze
/troubleshoot
```

### Other Orchestrators

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| claude-flow | Multi-agent orchestration |
| claude-squad | Team coordination |
| ccpm | Claude Code Package Manager |

---

## Subagents Collections

### Official Structure

Location: `.claude/agents/` (project) or `~/.claude/agents/` (global)

**Configuration Format:**
```yaml
---
name: agent-name
description: When to use this agent
tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep]
model: sonnet  # or opus, haiku, inherit
permissionMode: default
skills: [optional-skills]
---

System prompt content here...
```

**Model Options:**
- `sonnet` - Default, balanced
- `opus` - Most capable
- `haiku` - Fastest, cheapest
- `inherit` - Use parent model

### Popular Collections

| Repository | Agents | Focus |
|------------|--------|-------|
| [VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents](https://github.com/VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents) | 100+ | Full-stack, DevOps, data science |
| [wshobson/agents](https://github.com/wshobson/agents) | 91 | 15 orchestrators, 47 skills, 45 tools |
| [0xfurai/claude-code-subagents](https://github.com/0xfurai/claude-code-subagents) | 100+ | Production-ready, domain experts |
| [lst97/claude-code-sub-agents](https://github.com/lst97/claude-code-sub-agents) | - | Full-stack development |
| [zhsama/claude-sub-agent](https://github.com/zhsama/claude-sub-agent) | - | Phased workflow system |
| [vanzan01/claude-code-sub-agent-collective](https://github.com/vanzan01/claude-code-sub-agent-collective) | - | Context engineering, hub-spoke coordination |

### Tool Assignment Guidelines

| Agent Type | Recommended Tools |
|------------|-------------------|
| Read-only/Research | Read, Grep, Glob |
| Web Research | WebFetch, WebSearch |
| Code Writers | Read, Write, Edit, Bash |
| Full Access | All tools |

---

## Hooks & Automation

### Hook Types

| Hook | Trigger | Use Cases |
|------|---------|-----------|
| PreToolUse | Before any tool executes | Validation, blocking |
| PostToolUse | After tool completes | Logging, formatting |
| Stop | Session ends | Cleanup, notifications |
| SessionStart | Session begins | Environment setup |
| UserPromptSubmit | User submits prompt | Input validation |

### Configuration Locations

- **Global:** `~/.claude/settings.json`
- **Project:** `.claude/settings.json`

### Example: Pre-Commit Linting

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Bash(git commit:*)",
        "command": "~/.claude/hooks/pre-commit.sh"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

### Common Hook Use Cases

**Pre-Commit:**
- Run linters (ESLint, Prettier, Rubocop)
- Run tests before commit
- Secret scanning
- TODO/FIXME detection
- Automatic ticket ID association
- Co-author trailer addition

**Post-Commit:**
- Notifications
- Sync with external services
- Logging

**Blocking Protected Files:**
```python
# Return exit code 2 to block
if file_path in ['.env', 'package-lock.json']:
    sys.exit(2)
```

### Headless Mode for CI

```bash
claude -p "Your prompt" --output-format stream-json
```

---

## Slash Commands

### Built-in Commands

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `/init` | Generate CLAUDE.md from project analysis |
| `/clear` | Clear context, start fresh |
| `/compact` | Summarize conversation (auto at 80%) |
| `/memory` | Manage CLAUDE.md content |
| `/output-style` | Change response formatting |
| `/statusline` | Configure status line |
| `/terminal-setup` | Fix terminal keybindings |
| `/plugin` | Browse/install plugins |
| `/install-github-app` | Enable PR auto-review |

### Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/` (project) or `~/.claude/commands/` (global)

**Format:** Markdown files that become slash commands

```markdown
<!-- .claude/commands/debug-loop.md -->
# Debug Loop

Analyze the error, propose fixes, and test until resolved.
Focus on: $ARGUMENTS
```

**Usage:** `/debug-loop TypeError in auth module`

### Popular Command Collections

- [awattar/claude-code-best-practices](https://github.com/awattar/claude-code-best-practices) - `/custom-init`, `/help-commands`
- SuperClaude - 25 specialized commands

---

## CLAUDE.md Best Practices

### File Locations

| Location | Scope | Use For |
|----------|-------|---------|
| `./CLAUDE.md` | Project | Architecture, standards, deployment |
| `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Global | Personal preferences, workflows |

### Key Principles

1. **Initialize with `/init`** - Generates from project analysis
2. **Iterate and refine** - Like any frequently used prompt
3. **Keep concise** - Human-readable, no fluff
4. **Use `#` key** - Add instructions you repeat often
5. **Share via git** - Project CLAUDE.md benefits whole team

### Recommended Content

```markdown
# Project Name

## Architecture
- Key patterns and decisions
- Folder structure explanation

## Coding Standards
- Style guide references
- Naming conventions

## Common Commands
- Build: `npm run build`
- Test: `npm test`
- Deploy: `./deploy.sh`

## Important Files
- Entry point: src/index.ts
- Config: config/settings.ts

## Things That Work
- Pattern X for problem Y

## Things That Don't Work
- Avoid pattern Z because...
```

### Performance Impact

- Repository-specific optimization: **+11% better code**
- System prompt optimization: **+5% general performance**

### Key Resources

- [Cranot/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/Cranot/claude-code-guide)
- [zebbern/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/zebbern/claude-code-guide)
- [awattar/claude-code-best-practices](https://github.com/awattar/claude-code-best-practices)
- [Official Blog](https://claude.com/blog/using-claude-md-files)

---

## Status Line Tools

### Official Configuration

**Location:** `.claude/settings.json`

```json
{
  "statusLine": {
    "type": "command",
    "command": "~/.claude/statusline.sh",
    "padding": 0
  }
}
```

Claude passes session JSON via stdin (model, directories, git info).

### Popular Tools

| Tool | Stars | Features |
|------|-------|----------|
| [sirmalloc/ccstatusline](https://github.com/sirmalloc/ccstatusline) | 2.3k | Multi-line, TUI config, themes, powerline |
| [rz1989s/claude-code-statusline](https://github.com/rz1989s/claude-code-statusline) | - | Cost tracking, MCP monitoring, themes |
| [chongdashu/cc-statusline](https://github.com/chongdashu/cc-statusline) | - | Bash-first, zero dependencies |
| [gabriel-dehan/claude_monitor_statusline](https://github.com/gabriel-dehan/claude_monitor_statusline) | - | Usage info display |

### Installation (ccstatusline)

```bash
npx ccstatusline@latest
# or
bunx ccstatusline@latest
```

### Key Metrics to Display

- **Context usage %** - Most important (stop around 60%)
- Model name
- Current directory
- Git branch
- Uncommitted file count
- Token usage progress bar
- Cost tracking

**Why Monitor Context:**
- 200K token window
- Auto-compacts at 80%
- Performance degrades as context fills

---

## Output Styles

### Configuration

```json
{
  "outputStyle": "default"
}
```

### Custom Styles

**Locations:**
- `~/.claude/output-styles/` (global)
- `.claude/output-styles/` (project)

**Selection:** `/output-style` command

**Persistence:** Stored in `.claude/settings.local.json`

### Verbose Mode

```bash
claude --verbose  # Detailed logging for debugging
```

---

## MCP Servers

### Recommended Servers

| Server | Package | Purpose |
|--------|---------|---------|
| sequential-thinking | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking` | Complex reasoning |
| playwright | `@playwright/mcp@latest` | Browser automation |
| filesystem | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem` | File operations |
| sqlite | `uvx mcp-server-sqlite` | Database queries |
| memory | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-memory` | Persistent memory |
| fetch | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-fetch` | Web fetching |
| puppeteer | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-puppeteer` | Browser control |
| brave-search | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search` | Web search |

### Configuration

**Location:** `~/.claude.json`

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "sequential-thinking": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Lazy Loading

Set `"enableAllProjectMcpServers": false` in settings to only load MCP servers when explicitly called.

---

## IDE Integrations

| IDE | Extension | Features |
|-----|-----------|----------|
| VS Code | Official Claude Code | Inline diffs, real-time edits |
| VS Code | Claude Code Chat | Native chat interface, history |
| Neovim | claudecode.nvim | Pure Lua integration |
| Cursor | Claude-Autopilot | Task automation |
| GitButler | Hooks integration | Branch management |

---

## Tips & Tricks

### Permission Management

```bash
# Skip all permission prompts (use carefully)
claude --dangerously-skip-permissions

# Pre-approve common commands
claude allow "git commit"
claude allow "npm install"
```

### Keyboard Shortcuts

| Action | Key |
|--------|-----|
| Stop Claude | `Escape` |
| Previous messages | `Escape` twice |
| Paste images | `Ctrl+V` (not Cmd+V) |
| New line | Run `/terminal-setup` first |

### Thinking Depth

| Phrase | Depth |
|--------|-------|
| "think" | Standard analysis |
| "think hard" | Deeper analysis |
| "ultrathink" | Maximum depth |

### Context Management

- **Start fresh:** `/clear` regularly
- **Auto-compact:** Happens at 80% context
- **Monitor:** Use status line for context %
- **Subagents:** Use for complex tasks to preserve main context

### Parallel Sessions

```bash
# Use git worktree for parallel branches
git worktree add ../feature-branch feature-branch
cd ../feature-branch
claude  # Separate session
```

### Best Practices

1. **Read before modifying** - Never propose changes to unread code
2. **Use subagents early** - Preserves main context
3. **Clear prompts** - Use XML-like tags for structure
4. **Structured prompting** - Specify exact tasks, files, outputs
5. **Fresh sessions** - Reduce prompt-drift

---

## Key Resources

### Official Documentation
- [Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs)
- [Best Practices](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices)
- [CLAUDE.md Guide](https://claude.com/blog/using-claude-md-files)
- [Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

### Community Guides
- [ClaudeLog](https://claudelog.com/) - Docs, tutorials, best practices
- [awesomeclaude.ai](https://awesomeclaude.ai/) - Comprehensive resource hub
- [32 Claude Code Tips](https://agenticcoding.substack.com/p/32-claude-code-tips-from-basics-to)
- [Cooking with Claude Code](https://www.siddharthbharath.com/claude-code-the-complete-guide/)

### GitHub Guides
- [Cranot/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/Cranot/claude-code-guide)
- [zebbern/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/zebbern/claude-code-guide)
- [wesammustafa/Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know](https://github.com/wesammustafa/Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know)

### Blog Posts
- [Demystifying Claude Code Hooks](https://www.brethorsting.com/blog/2025/08/demystifying-claude-code-hooks/)
- [Hooks for Automated Quality Checks](https://www.letanure.dev/blog/2025-08-06--claude-code-part-8-hooks-automated-quality-checks)
- [Optimizing CLAUDE.md](https://arize.com/blog/claude-md-best-practices-learned-from-optimizing-claude-code-with-prompt-learning/)

---

## Quick Reference

### File Locations Summary

| Purpose | Project Location | Global Location |
|---------|------------------|-----------------|
| Memory/Context | `./CLAUDE.md` | `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` |
| Settings | `.claude/settings.json` | `~/.claude/settings.json` |
| Local Settings | `.claude/settings.local.json` | `~/.claude/settings.local.json` |
| Slash Commands | `.claude/commands/` | `~/.claude/commands/` |
| Subagents | `.claude/agents/` | `~/.claude/agents/` |
| Skills | `.claude/skills/` | `~/.claude/skills/` |
| Output Styles | `.claude/output-styles/` | `~/.claude/output-styles/` |
| MCP Config | - | `~/.claude.json` |

### Essential Commands

```bash
# Initialize project
/init

# Clear context
/clear

# Configure status line
/statusline

# Configure output style
/output-style

# Browse plugins
/plugin

# Setup terminal
/terminal-setup
```

---

*This document is a living reference. Update as new tools and practices emerge.*

## Subagents Collections

### Official Structure

Location: `.claude/agents/` (project) or `~/.claude/agents/` (global)

**Configuration Format:**
```yaml
---
name: agent-name
description: When to use this agent
tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep]
model: sonnet  # or opus, haiku, inherit
permissionMode: default
skills: [optional-skills]
---

System prompt content here...
```

**Model Options:**
- `sonnet` - Default, balanced
- `opus` - Most capable
- `haiku` - Fastest, cheapest
- `inherit` - Use parent model

### Popular Collections

| Repository | Agents | Focus |
|------------|--------|-------|
| [VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents](https://github.com/VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents) | 100+ | Full-stack, DevOps, data science |
| [wshobson/agents](https://github.com/wshobson/agents) | 91 | 15 orchestrators, 47 skills, 45 tools |
| [0xfurai/claude-code-subagents](https://github.com/0xfurai/claude-code-subagents) | 100+ | Production-ready, domain experts |
| [lst97/claude-code-sub-agents](https://github.com/lst97/claude-code-sub-agents) | - | Full-stack development |
| [zhsama/claude-sub-agent](https://github.com/zhsama/claude-sub-agent) | - | Phased workflow system |
| [vanzan01/claude-code-sub-agent-collective](https://github.com/vanzan01/claude-code-sub-agent-collective) | - | Context engineering, hub-spoke coordination |

### Tool Assignment Guidelines

| Agent Type | Recommended Tools |
|------------|-------------------|
| Read-only/Research | Read, Grep, Glob |
| Web Research | WebFetch, WebSearch |
| Code Writers | Read, Write, Edit, Bash |
| Full Access | All tools |

---

## Hooks & Automation

### Hook Types

| Hook | Trigger | Use Cases |
|------|---------|-----------|
| PreToolUse | Before any tool executes | Validation, blocking |
| PostToolUse | After tool completes | Logging, formatting |
| Stop | Session ends | Cleanup, notifications |
| SessionStart | Session begins | Environment setup |
| UserPromptSubmit | User submits prompt | Input validation |

### Configuration Locations

- **Global:** `~/.claude/settings.json`
- **Project:** `.claude/settings.json`

### Example: Pre-Commit Linting

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Bash(git commit:*)",
        "command": "~/.claude/hooks/pre-commit.sh"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

### Common Hook Use Cases

**Pre-Commit:**
- Run linters (ESLint, Prettier, Rubocop)
- Run tests before commit
- Secret scanning
- TODO/FIXME detection
- Automatic ticket ID association
- Co-author trailer addition

**Post-Commit:**
- Notifications
- Sync with external services
- Logging

**Blocking Protected Files:**
```python
# Return exit code 2 to block
if file_path in ['.env', 'package-lock.json']:
    sys.exit(2)
```

### Headless Mode for CI

```bash
claude -p "Your prompt" --output-format stream-json
```

---

## Slash Commands

### Built-in Commands

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `/init` | Generate CLAUDE.md from project analysis |
| `/clear` | Clear context, start fresh |
| `/compact` | Summarize conversation (auto at 80%) |
| `/memory` | Manage CLAUDE.md content |
| `/output-style` | Change response formatting |
| `/statusline` | Configure status line |
| `/terminal-setup` | Fix terminal keybindings |
| `/plugin` | Browse/install plugins |
| `/install-github-app` | Enable PR auto-review |

### Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/` (project) or `~/.claude/commands/` (global)

**Format:** Markdown files that become slash commands

```markdown
<!-- .claude/commands/debug-loop.md -->
# Debug Loop

Analyze the error, propose fixes, and test until resolved.
Focus on: $ARGUMENTS
```

**Usage:** `/debug-loop TypeError in auth module`

### Popular Command Collections

- [awattar/claude-code-best-practices](https://github.com/awattar/claude-code-best-practices) - `/custom-init`, `/help-commands`
- SuperClaude - 25 specialized commands

---

## CLAUDE.md Best Practices

### File Locations

| Location | Scope | Use For |
|----------|-------|---------|
| `./CLAUDE.md` | Project | Architecture, standards, deployment |
| `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Global | Personal preferences, workflows |

### Key Principles

1. **Initialize with `/init`** - Generates from project analysis
2. **Iterate and refine** - Like any frequently used prompt
3. **Keep concise** - Human-readable, no fluff
4. **Use `#` key** - Add instructions you repeat often
5. **Share via git** - Project CLAUDE.md benefits whole team

### Recommended Content

```markdown
# Project Name

## Architecture
- Key patterns and decisions
- Folder structure explanation

## Coding Standards
- Style guide references
- Naming conventions

## Common Commands
- Build: `npm run build`
- Test: `npm test`
- Deploy: `./deploy.sh`

## Important Files
- Entry point: src/index.ts
- Config: config/settings.ts

## Things That Work
- Pattern X for problem Y

## Things That Don't Work
- Avoid pattern Z because...
```

### Performance Impact

- Repository-specific optimization: **+11% better code**
- System prompt optimization: **+5% general performance**

### Key Resources

- [Cranot/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/Cranot/claude-code-guide)
- [zebbern/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/zebbern/claude-code-guide)
- [awattar/claude-code-best-practices](https://github.com/awattar/claude-code-best-practices)
- [Official Blog](https://claude.com/blog/using-claude-md-files)

---

## Status Line Tools

### Official Configuration

**Location:** `.claude/settings.json`

```json
{
  "statusLine": {
    "type": "command",
    "command": "~/.claude/statusline.sh",
    "padding": 0
  }
}
```

Claude passes session JSON via stdin (model, directories, git info).

### Popular Tools

| Tool | Stars | Features |
|------|-------|----------|
| [sirmalloc/ccstatusline](https://github.com/sirmalloc/ccstatusline) | 2.3k | Multi-line, TUI config, themes, powerline |
| [rz1989s/claude-code-statusline](https://github.com/rz1989s/claude-code-statusline) | - | Cost tracking, MCP monitoring, themes |
| [chongdashu/cc-statusline](https://github.com/chongdashu/cc-statusline) | - | Bash-first, zero dependencies |
| [gabriel-dehan/claude_monitor_statusline](https://github.com/gabriel-dehan/claude_monitor_statusline) | - | Usage info display |

### Installation (ccstatusline)

```bash
npx ccstatusline@latest
# or
bunx ccstatusline@latest
```

### Key Metrics to Display

- **Context usage %** - Most important (stop around 60%)
- Model name
- Current directory
- Git branch
- Uncommitted file count
- Token usage progress bar
- Cost tracking

**Why Monitor Context:**
- 200K token window
- Auto-compacts at 80%
- Performance degrades as context fills

---

## Output Styles

### Configuration

```json
{
  "outputStyle": "default"
}
```

### Custom Styles

**Locations:**
- `~/.claude/output-styles/` (global)
- `.claude/output-styles/` (project)

**Selection:** `/output-style` command

**Persistence:** Stored in `.claude/settings.local.json`

### Verbose Mode

```bash
claude --verbose  # Detailed logging for debugging
```

## MCP Servers

### Recommended Servers

| Server | Package | Purpose |
|--------|---------|---------|
| sequential-thinking | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking` | Complex reasoning |
| playwright | `@playwright/mcp@latest` | Browser automation |
| filesystem | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem` | File operations |
| sqlite | `uvx mcp-server-sqlite` | Database queries |
| memory | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-memory` | Persistent memory |
| fetch | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-fetch` | Web fetching |
| puppeteer | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-puppeteer` | Browser control |
| brave-search | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search` | Web search |

### Configuration

**Location:** `~/.claude.json`

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "sequential-thinking": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Lazy Loading

Set `"enableAllProjectMcpServers": false` in settings to only load MCP servers when explicitly called.

## IDE Integrations

| IDE | Extension | Features |
|-----|-----------|----------|
| VS Code | Official Claude Code | Inline diffs, real-time edits |
| VS Code | Claude Code Chat | Native chat interface, history |
| Neovim | claudecode.nvim | Pure Lua integration |
| Cursor | Claude-Autopilot | Task automation |
| GitButler | Hooks integration | Branch management |

---

## Tips & Tricks

### Permission Management

```bash
# Skip all permission prompts (use carefully)
claude --dangerously-skip-permissions

# Pre-approve common commands
claude allow "git commit"
claude allow "npm install"
```

### Keyboard Shortcuts

| Action | Key |
|--------|-----|
| Stop Claude | `Escape` |
| Previous messages | `Escape` twice |
| Paste images | `Ctrl+V` (not Cmd+V) |
| New line | Run `/terminal-setup` first |

### Thinking Depth

| Phrase | Depth |
|--------|-------|
| "think" | Standard analysis |
| "think hard" | Deeper analysis |
| "ultrathink" | Maximum depth |

### Context Management

- **Start fresh:** `/clear` regularly
- **Auto-compact:** Happens at 80% context
- **Monitor:** Use status line for context %
- **Subagents:** Use for complex tasks to preserve main context

### Parallel Sessions

```bash
# Use git worktree for parallel branches
git worktree add ../feature-branch feature-branch
cd ../feature-branch
claude  # Separate session
```

### Best Practices

1. **Read before modifying** - Never propose changes to unread code
2. **Use subagents early** - Preserves main context
3. **Clear prompts** - Use XML-like tags for structure
4. **Structured prompting** - Specify exact tasks, files, outputs
5. **Fresh sessions** - Reduce prompt-drift

input.new_string:
---

## Installation Plan & Recommendations

### What to Install

| Resource | Recommendation | Reasoning |
|----------|---------------|-----------|
| **ccstatusline** | **INSTALL** | Essential for context monitoring. Simple npx setup. |
| **SuperClaude Framework** | **SKIP** | Conflicts with taches-cc-resources plugin. Complex pipx setup. |
| **100+ Subagent Collections** | **SKIP** | Already have good generic agents from plugins. Would bloat context. |
| **Additional MCP Servers** | **AS NEEDED** | Only add specific servers when a project requires them. |
| **More Slash Commands** | **SELECTIVE** | Browse awesome lists for specific needs. |
| **Custom Hooks** | **ADD GRADUALLY** | Start with pre-commit linting, expand as needed. |

### Recommended Installation Steps

#### Step 1: Install ccstatusline (Optional but Recommended)
```bash
npx ccstatusline@latest
```
- Provides real-time context usage monitoring
- Helps prevent performance degradation from context bloat
- Stop working around 60% context usage

#### Step 2: Add Pre-Commit Hook (Optional)
Create `~/.claude/hooks/pre-commit.sh`:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Run linter on staged files
npm run lint --quiet || exit 2
```

Add to `~/.claude/settings.json`:
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [{
      "matcher": "Bash(git commit:*)",
      "command": "~/.claude/hooks/pre-commit.sh"
    }]
  }
}
```

#### Step 3: Add MCP Servers as Needed
Current recommended setup (already configured):
- `sequential-thinking` - Complex reasoning chains
- `playwright` - Browser automation/testing
- `memory` - Persistent memory across sessions
- `sqlite` - Database operations
- `filesystem` - File operations (scoped to `.`)

### What NOT to Install

| Resource | Why Skip |
|----------|----------|
| **Multiple agent collections** | Context bloat, conflicting patterns |
| **SuperClaude + taches-cc-resources** | Command conflicts |
| **All 243 plugins** | Most are unused, slows startup |
| **GitHub MCP** | Requires API key management, permissions unclear |

### Philosophy: Less is More

- Each skill/agent/command loads into context window
- More tools = less room for actual work
- Add tools only when you have a specific use case
- Remove unused tools periodically

---

## Portable .claude Folder Guide

### Files to INCLUDE When Copying

| Path | Purpose | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| `settings.json` | Permissions, plugins | Core config |
| `settings.local.json` | Local overrides | Usually same as settings.json |
| `commands/` | Custom slash commands | Your workflow |
| `skills/` | Custom skills | Domain knowledge |
| `templates/` | Task templates | Workflows |
| `plans/` | Planning documents | Reference |
| `automation-guide.md` | Workflow docs | Reference |
| `workflow-checklist.md` | Checklist | Reference |
| `CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md` | This doc | Reference |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Global memory | Personal preferences |

### Files to EXCLUDE When Copying

| Path | Why Exclude |
|------|-------------|
| `.claude/` (nested) | Accidental duplicate |
| `debug/` | Session logs |
| `file-history/` | Machine-specific edit history |
| `ide/` | IDE lock files |
| `projects/` | Machine-specific path hashes |
| `shell-snapshots/` | Shell state backups |
| `statsig/` | Analytics cache |
| `telemetry/` | Telemetry data |
| `todos/` | Old agent task data |
| `security_warnings_*.json` | Session-specific |
| `history.jsonl` | Conversation history |
| `stats-cache.json` | Usage statistics |
| `.credentials.json` | Authentication |
| `sqlite_mcp_server.db` | Local MCP database |

### MCP Server Configuration

The `~/.claude.json` file is NOT inside `.claude/` - it lives at `~/.claude.json`.

**For portability**, create a template MCP config:

**File: `.claude/mcp-servers-template.json`**
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "sequential-thinking": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"]
    },
    "playwright": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@playwright/mcp@latest"]
    },
    "memory": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-memory"]
    },
    "filesystem": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "."]
    }
  }
}
```

**On a new machine**, merge this into `~/.claude.json`.

### Copying to a New Location

```bash
# From source machine
cd ~/.claude
# Create portable archive (excluding machine-specific files)
tar --exclude='debug' \
    --exclude='file-history' \
    --exclude='ide' \
    --exclude='projects' \
    --exclude='shell-snapshots' \
    --exclude='statsig' \
    --exclude='telemetry' \
    --exclude='todos' \
    --exclude='downloads' \
    --exclude='security_warnings_*' \
    --exclude='history.jsonl' \
    --exclude='stats-cache.json' \
    --exclude='.credentials.json' \
    --exclude='sqlite_mcp_server.db' \
    --exclude='.claude' \
    -cvf claude-portable.tar .

# On destination machine
mkdir -p ~/.claude
cd ~/.claude
tar -xvf /path/to/claude-portable.tar

# Merge MCP servers into ~/.claude.json if needed
```

### For Windows (PowerShell)

```powershell
# Create list of files to copy
$include = @(
    'settings.json',
    'settings.local.json',
    'commands',
    'skills',
    'templates',
    'plans',
    'plugins',
    'automation-guide.md',
    'workflow-checklist.md',
    'CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md',
    'CLAUDE.md'
)

# Copy to destination
$dest = "D:\path\to\destination\.claude"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dest
foreach ($item in $include) {
    $src = Join-Path "C:\Users\tjn\.claude" $item
    if (Test-Path $src) {
        Copy-Item -Path $src -Destination $dest -Recurse -Force
    }
}
```

toolUseResult.newString:
---

## Installation Plan & Recommendations

### What to Install

| Resource | Recommendation | Reasoning |
|----------|---------------|-----------|
| **ccstatusline** | **INSTALL** | Essential for context monitoring. Simple npx setup. |
| **SuperClaude Framework** | **SKIP** | Conflicts with taches-cc-resources plugin. Complex pipx setup. |
| **100+ Subagent Collections** | **SKIP** | Already have good generic agents from plugins. Would bloat context. |
| **Additional MCP Servers** | **AS NEEDED** | Only add specific servers when a project requires them. |
| **More Slash Commands** | **SELECTIVE** | Browse awesome lists for specific needs. |
| **Custom Hooks** | **ADD GRADUALLY** | Start with pre-commit linting, expand as needed. |

### Recommended Installation Steps

#### Step 1: Install ccstatusline (Optional but Recommended)
```bash
npx ccstatusline@latest
```
- Provides real-time context usage monitoring
- Helps prevent performance degradation from context bloat
- Stop working around 60% context usage

#### Step 2: Add Pre-Commit Hook (Optional)
Create `~/.claude/hooks/pre-commit.sh`:
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Run linter on staged files
npm run lint --quiet || exit 2
```

Add to `~/.claude/settings.json`:
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [{
      "matcher": "Bash(git commit:*)",
      "command": "~/.claude/hooks/pre-commit.sh"
    }]
  }
}
```

#### Step 3: Add MCP Servers as Needed
Current recommended setup (already configured):
- `sequential-thinking` - Complex reasoning chains
- `playwright` - Browser automation/testing
- `memory` - Persistent memory across sessions
- `sqlite` - Database operations
- `filesystem` - File operations (scoped to `.`)

### What NOT to Install

| Resource | Why Skip |
|----------|----------|
| **Multiple agent collections** | Context bloat, conflicting patterns |
| **SuperClaude + taches-cc-resources** | Command conflicts |
| **All 243 plugins** | Most are unused, slows startup |
| **GitHub MCP** | Requires API key management, permissions unclear |

### Philosophy: Less is More

- Each skill/agent/command loads into context window
- More tools = less room for actual work
- Add tools only when you have a specific use case
- Remove unused tools periodically

---

## Portable .claude Folder Guide

### Files to INCLUDE When Copying

| Path | Purpose | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| `settings.json` | Permissions, plugins | Core config |
| `settings.local.json` | Local overrides | Usually same as settings.json |
| `commands/` | Custom slash commands | Your workflow |
| `skills/` | Custom skills | Domain knowledge |
| `templates/` | Task templates | Workflows |
| `plans/` | Planning documents | Reference |
| `automation-guide.md` | Workflow docs | Reference |
| `workflow-checklist.md` | Checklist | Reference |
| `CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md` | This doc | Reference |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Global memory | Personal preferences |

### Files to EXCLUDE When Copying

| Path | Why Exclude |
|------|-------------|
| `.claude/` (nested) | Accidental duplicate |
| `debug/` | Session logs |
| `file-history/` | Machine-specific edit history |
| `ide/` | IDE lock files |
| `projects/` | Machine-specific path hashes |
| `shell-snapshots/` | Shell state backups |
| `statsig/` | Analytics cache |
| `telemetry/` | Telemetry data |
| `todos/` | Old agent task data |
| `security_warnings_*.json` | Session-specific |
| `history.jsonl` | Conversation history |
| `stats-cache.json` | Usage statistics |
| `.credentials.json` | Authentication |
| `sqlite_mcp_server.db` | Local MCP database |

### MCP Server Configuration

The `~/.claude.json` file is NOT inside `.claude/` - it lives at `~/.claude.json`.

**For portability**, create a template MCP config:

**File: `.claude/mcp-servers-template.json`**
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "sequential-thinking": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"]
    },
    "playwright": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@playwright/mcp@latest"]
    },
    "memory": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-memory"]
    },
    "filesystem": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "."]
    }
  }
}
```

**On a new machine**, merge this into `~/.claude.json`.

### Copying to a New Location

```bash
# From source machine
cd ~/.claude
# Create portable archive (excluding machine-specific files)
tar --exclude='debug' \
    --exclude='file-history' \
    --exclude='ide' \
    --exclude='projects' \
    --exclude='shell-snapshots' \
    --exclude='statsig' \
    --exclude='telemetry' \
    --exclude='todos' \
    --exclude='downloads' \
    --exclude='security_warnings_*' \
    --exclude='history.jsonl' \
    --exclude='stats-cache.json' \
    --exclude='.credentials.json' \
    --exclude='sqlite_mcp_server.db' \
    --exclude='.claude' \
    -cvf claude-portable.tar .

# On destination machine
mkdir -p ~/.claude
cd ~/.claude
tar -xvf /path/to/claude-portable.tar

# Merge MCP servers into ~/.claude.json if needed
```

### For Windows (PowerShell)

```powershell
# Create list of files to copy
$include = @(
    'settings.json',
    'settings.local.json',
    'commands',
    'skills',
    'templates',
    'plans',
    'plugins',
    'automation-guide.md',
    'workflow-checklist.md',
    'CLAUDE-CODE-RESOURCES.md',
    'CLAUDE.md'
)

# Copy to destination
$dest = "D:\path\to\destination\.claude"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dest
foreach ($item in $include) {
    $src = Join-Path "C:\Users\tjn\.claude" $item
    if (Test-Path $src) {
        Copy-Item -Path $src -Destination $dest -Recurse -Force
    }
}
```

---

*This document is a living reference. Update as new tools and practices emerge.*

# Claude Code Resources - Comprehensive Research Summary

**Compiled:** December 2025
**Purpose:** Reference guide for optimizing Claude Code utilization

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Awesome Lists & Plugin Collections](#awesome-lists--plugin-collections)
2. [Frameworks](#frameworks)
3. [Subagents Collections](#subagents-collections)
4. [Hooks & Automation](#hooks--automation)
5. [Slash Commands](#slash-commands)
6. [CLAUDE.md Best Practices](#claudemd-best-practices)
7. [Status Line Tools](#status-line-tools)
8. [Output Styles](#output-styles)
9. [MCP Servers](#mcp-servers)
10. [IDE Integrations](#ide-integrations)
11. [Tips & Tricks](#tips--tricks)
12. [Key Resources](#key-resources)
13. [Installation Plan & Recommendations](#installation-plan--recommendations)
14. [Portable .claude Folder Guide](#portable-claude-folder-guide)

---

## Awesome Lists & Plugin Collections

### Primary Collections

| Repository | Stars | Description |
|------------|-------|-------------|
| [ccplugins/awesome-claude-code-plugins](https://github.com/ccplugins/awesome-claude-code-plugins) | - | Curated list of slash commands, subagents, MCP servers, hooks |
| [jqueryscript/awesome-claude-code](https://github.com/jqueryscript/awesome-claude-code) | - | Tools, IDE integrations, frameworks, 100+ subagents |
| [jeremylongshore/claude-code-plugins-plus](https://github.com/jeremylongshore/claude-code-plugins-plus) | - | 243 plugins, 175 with Agent Skills v1.2.0 |
| [hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code](https://github.com/hesreallyhim/awesome-claude-code) | - | Hooks, slash commands, subagent files, workflows |
| [quemsah/awesome-claude-plugins](https://github.com/quemsah/awesome-claude-plugins) | - | 1,269 indexed repositories, automated metrics |
| [alvinunreal/awesome-claude](https://github.com/alvinunreal/awesome-claude) | - | General Claude resources |

### Official Plugin System (October 2025)
- Install plugins with `/plugin` command
- Works across terminal and VS Code
- Bundle slash commands, subagents, MCP servers, and hooks

---

## Frameworks

### SuperClaude Framework

**Repository:** [SuperClaude-Org/SuperClaude_Framework](https://github.com/SuperClaude-Org/SuperClaude_Framework)
**Website:** [superclaude.org](https://superclaude.org/)

**Features:**
- 25 specialized commands (`/sc:*` prefix)
- 9 Cognitive Personas with automatic activation
- Behavioral instruction injection
- Component orchestration

**Cognitive Personas:**
| Persona | Domain | Activation Triggers |
|---------|--------|---------------------|
| architect | System design | Architecture discussions |
| frontend | UI/UX | .tsx files, styling |
| backend | API/Database | Server code, APIs |
| security | Security audit | Security keywords |
| analyzer | Debugging | "bug", "error" keywords |
| qa | Testing | Test discussions |
| performance | Optimization | Performance issues |
| refactorer | Code cleanup | Refactoring tasks |
| mentor | Teaching | Explanations needed |

**Installation:**
```bash
pipx install superclaude
superclaude install
```

**Example Commands:**
```bash
/design --api --ddd --architect
/build --feature --tdd --backend
/test --coverage --e2e
/analyze
/troubleshoot
```

### Other Orchestrators

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| claude-flow | Multi-agent orchestration |
| claude-squad | Team coordination |
| ccpm | Claude Code Package Manager |

---

## Subagents Collections

### Official Structure

Location: `.claude/agents/` (project) or `~/.claude/agents/` (global)

**Configuration Format:**
```yaml
---
name: agent-name
description: When to use this agent
tools: [Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep]
model: sonnet  # or opus, haiku, inherit
permissionMode: default
skills: [optional-skills]
---

System prompt content here...
```

**Model Options:**
- `sonnet` - Default, balanced
- `opus` - Most capable
- `haiku` - Fastest, cheapest
- `inherit` - Use parent model

### Popular Collections

| Repository | Agents | Focus |
|------------|--------|-------|
| [VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents](https://github.com/VoltAgent/awesome-claude-code-subagents) | 100+ | Full-stack, DevOps, data science |
| [wshobson/agents](https://github.com/wshobson/agents) | 91 | 15 orchestrators, 47 skills, 45 tools |
| [0xfurai/claude-code-subagents](https://github.com/0xfurai/claude-code-subagents) | 100+ | Production-ready, domain experts |
| [lst97/claude-code-sub-agents](https://github.com/lst97/claude-code-sub-agents) | - | Full-stack development |
| [zhsama/claude-sub-agent](https://github.com/zhsama/claude-sub-agent) | - | Phased workflow system |
| [vanzan01/claude-code-sub-agent-collective](https://github.com/vanzan01/claude-code-sub-agent-collective) | - | Context engineering, hub-spoke coordination |

### Tool Assignment Guidelines

| Agent Type | Recommended Tools |
|------------|-------------------|
| Read-only/Research | Read, Grep, Glob |
| Web Research | WebFetch, WebSearch |
| Code Writers | Read, Write, Edit, Bash |
| Full Access | All tools |

---

## Hooks & Automation

### Hook Types

| Hook | Trigger | Use Cases |
|------|---------|-----------|
| PreToolUse | Before any tool executes | Validation, blocking |
| PostToolUse | After tool completes | Logging, formatting |
| Stop | Session ends | Cleanup, notifications |
| SessionStart | Session begins | Environment setup |
| UserPromptSubmit | User submits prompt | Input validation |

### Configuration Locations

- **Global:** `~/.claude/settings.json`
- **Project:** `.claude/settings.json`

### Example: Pre-Commit Linting

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [
      {
        "matcher": "Bash(git commit:*)",
        "command": "~/.claude/hooks/pre-commit.sh"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

### Common Hook Use Cases

**Pre-Commit:**
- Run linters (ESLint, Prettier, Rubocop)
- Run tests before commit
- Secret scanning
- TODO/FIXME detection
- Automatic ticket ID association
- Co-author trailer addition

**Post-Commit:**
- Notifications
- Sync with external services
- Logging

**Blocking Protected Files:**
```python
# Return exit code 2 to block
if file_path in ['.env', 'package-lock.json']:
    sys.exit(2)
```

### Headless Mode for CI

```bash
claude -p "Your prompt" --output-format stream-json
```

---

## Slash Commands

### Built-in Commands

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `/init` | Generate CLAUDE.md from project analysis |
| `/clear` | Clear context, start fresh |
| `/compact` | Summarize conversation (auto at 80%) |
| `/memory` | Manage CLAUDE.md content |
| `/output-style` | Change response formatting |
| `/statusline` | Configure status line |
| `/terminal-setup` | Fix terminal keybindings |
| `/plugin` | Browse/install plugins |
| `/install-github-app` | Enable PR auto-review |

### Custom Commands

**Location:** `.claude/commands/` (project) or `~/.claude/commands/` (global)

**Format:** Markdown files that become slash commands

```markdown
<!-- .claude/commands/debug-loop.md -->
# Debug Loop

Analyze the error, propose fixes, and test until resolved.
Focus on: $ARGUMENTS
```

**Usage:** `/debug-loop TypeError in auth module`

### Popular Command Collections

- [awattar/claude-code-best-practices](https://github.com/awattar/claude-code-best-practices) - `/custom-init`, `/help-commands`
- SuperClaude - 25 specialized commands

---

## CLAUDE.md Best Practices

### File Locations

| Location | Scope | Use For |
|----------|-------|---------|
| `./CLAUDE.md` | Project | Architecture, standards, deployment |
| `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` | Global | Personal preferences, workflows |

### Key Principles

1. **Initialize with `/init`** - Generates from project analysis
2. **Iterate and refine** - Like any frequently used prompt
3. **Keep concise** - Human-readable, no fluff
4. **Use `#` key** - Add instructions you repeat often
5. **Share via git** - Project CLAUDE.md benefits whole team

### Recommended Content

```markdown
# Project Name

## Architecture
- Key patterns and decisions
- Folder structure explanation

## Coding Standards
- Style guide references
- Naming conventions

## Common Commands
- Build: `npm run build`
- Test: `npm test`
- Deploy: `./deploy.sh`

## Important Files
- Entry point: src/index.ts
- Config: config/settings.ts

## Things That Work
- Pattern X for problem Y

## Things That Don't Work
- Avoid pattern Z because...
```

### Performance Impact

- Repository-specific optimization: **+11% better code**
- System prompt optimization: **+5% general performance**

### Key Resources

- [Cranot/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/Cranot/claude-code-guide)
- [zebbern/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/zebbern/claude-code-guide)
- [awattar/claude-code-best-practices](https://github.com/awattar/claude-code-best-practices)
- [Official Blog](https://claude.com/blog/using-claude-md-files)

---

## Status Line Tools

### Official Configuration

**Location:** `.claude/settings.json`

```json
{
  "statusLine": {
    "type": "command",
    "command": "~/.claude/statusline.sh",
    "padding": 0
  }
}
```

Claude passes session JSON via stdin (model, directories, git info).

### Popular Tools

| Tool | Stars | Features |
|------|-------|----------|
| [sirmalloc/ccstatusline](https://github.com/sirmalloc/ccstatusline) | 2.3k | Multi-line, TUI config, themes, powerline |
| [rz1989s/claude-code-statusline](https://github.com/rz1989s/claude-code-statusline) | - | Cost tracking, MCP monitoring, themes |
| [chongdashu/cc-statusline](https://github.com/chongdashu/cc-statusline) | - | Bash-first, zero dependencies |
| [gabriel-dehan/claude_monitor_statusline](https://github.com/gabriel-dehan/claude_monitor_statusline) | - | Usage info display |

### Installation (ccstatusline)

```bash
npx ccstatusline@latest
# or
bunx ccstatusline@latest
```

### Key Metrics to Display

- **Context usage %** - Most important (stop around 60%)
- Model name
- Current directory
- Git branch
- Uncommitted file count
- Token usage progress bar
- Cost tracking

**Why Monitor Context:**
- 200K token window
- Auto-compacts at 80%
- Performance degrades as context fills

---

## Output Styles

### Configuration

```json
{
  "outputStyle": "default"
}
```

### Custom Styles

**Locations:**
- `~/.claude/output-styles/` (global)
- `.claude/output-styles/` (project)

**Selection:** `/output-style` command

**Persistence:** Stored in `.claude/settings.local.json`

### Verbose Mode

```bash
claude --verbose  # Detailed logging for debugging
```

---

## MCP Servers

### Recommended Servers

| Server | Package | Purpose |
|--------|---------|---------|
| sequential-thinking | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking` | Complex reasoning |
| playwright | `@playwright/mcp@latest` | Browser automation |
| filesystem | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem` | File operations |
| sqlite | `uvx mcp-server-sqlite` | Database queries |
| memory | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-memory` | Persistent memory |
| fetch | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-fetch` | Web fetching |
| puppeteer | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-puppeteer` | Browser control |
| brave-search | `@modelcontextprotocol/server-brave-search` | Web search |

### Configuration

**Location:** `~/.claude.json`

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "sequential-thinking": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Lazy Loading

Set `"enableAllProjectMcpServers": false` in settings to only load MCP servers when explicitly called.

---

## IDE Integrations

| IDE | Extension | Features |
|-----|-----------|----------|
| VS Code | Official Claude Code | Inline diffs, real-time edits |
| VS Code | Claude Code Chat | Native chat interface, history |
| Neovim | claudecode.nvim | Pure Lua integration |
| Cursor | Claude-Autopilot | Task automation |
| GitButler | Hooks integration | Branch management |

---

## Tips & Tricks

### Permission Management

```bash
# Skip all permission prompts (use carefully)
claude --dangerously-skip-permissions

# Pre-approve common commands
claude allow "git commit"
claude allow "npm install"
```

### Keyboard Shortcuts

| Action | Key |
|--------|-----|
| Stop Claude | `Escape` |
| Previous messages | `Escape` twice |
| Paste images | `Ctrl+V` (not Cmd+V) |
| New line | Run `/terminal-setup` first |

### Thinking Depth

| Phrase | Depth |
|--------|-------|
| "think" | Standard analysis |
| "think hard" | Deeper analysis |
| "ultrathink" | Maximum depth |

### Context Management

- **Start fresh:** `/clear` regularly
- **Auto-compact:** Happens at 80% context
- **Monitor:** Use status line for context %
- **Subagents:** Use for complex tasks to preserve main context

### Parallel Sessions

```bash
# Use git worktree for parallel branches
git worktree add ../feature-branch feature-branch
cd ../feature-branch
claude  # Separate session
```

### Best Practices

1. **Read before modifying** - Never propose changes to unread code
2. **Use subagents early** - Preserves main context
3. **Clear prompts** - Use XML-like tags for structure
4. **Structured prompting** - Specify exact tasks, files, outputs
5. **Fresh sessions** - Reduce prompt-drift

---

## Key Resources

- [Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs)
- [Best Practices](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices)

- [awesomeclaude.ai](https://awesomeclaude.ai/) - Comprehensive resource hub
- [32 Claude Code Tips](https://agenticcoding.substack.com/p/32-claude-code-tips-from-basics-to)
- [Cooking with Claude Code](https://www.siddharthbharath.com/claude-code-the-complete-guide/)

### GitHub Guides
- [Cranot/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/Cranot/claude-code-guide)
- [zebbern/claude-code-guide](https://github.com/zebbern/claude-code-guide)
- [wesammustafa/Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know](https://github.com/wesammustafa/Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know)

- [Optimizing CLAUDE.md](https://arize.com/blog/claude-md-best-practices-learned-from-optimizing-claude-code-with-prompt-learning/)

---

## Quick Reference

### File Locations Summary

| Purpose | Project Location | Global Location |
|---------|------------------|-----------------|
| Memory/Context | `./CLAUDE.md` | `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` |
| Settings | `.claude/settings.json` | `~/.claude/settings.json` |
| Local Settings | `.claude/settings.local.json` | `~/.claude/settings.local.json` |
| Slash Commands | `.claude/commands/` | `~/.claude/commands/` |
| Subagents | `.claude/agents/` | `~/.claude/agents/` |
| Skills | `.claude/skills/` | `~/.claude/skills/` |
| Output Styles | `.claude/output-styles/` | `~/.claude/output-styles/` |
| MCP Config | - | `~/.claude.json` |

### Essential Commands

```bash
# Initialize project
/init

# Clear context
/clear

# Configure status line
/statusline

# Configure output style
/output-style

# Browse plugins
/plugin

# Setup terminal

file.content:
# Claude Code Skills Library

Custom skills for Claude Code - reusable across any project.

**Version:** 2.0 (Merged & Generic)
**Last Updated:** December 2025

---

## Generic Skills (Use for Any Project)

### Development Skills

| Skill | Purpose | When to Use |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| **generic-code-reviewer** | Multi-stack code review | Before commits, PR reviews |
| **generic-feature-developer** | Architecture patterns | Adding features, planning changes |
| **generic-design-system** | Design tokens & patterns | UI implementation, styling |
| **generic-ux-designer** | UX best practices | Design reviews, user flows |

### Utility Skills

| Skill | Purpose | When to Use |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| **codebase-documenter** | Generate documentation | README, API docs, comments |
| **tech-debt-analyzer** | Code health analysis | Audits, refactoring planning |
| **test-specialist** | Testing guidance | Writing tests, debugging |
| **frontend-enhancer** | Frontend patterns | UI improvements |
| **document-skills** | Office docs (docx, xlsx, pptx, pdf) | Document creation |
| **seo-analytics-auditor** | SEO analysis | Performance audits |

---

## Project-Specific Skills (Examples)

These skills show how to customize for specific projects:

### tdr-* (thedeadrobot.com)
- **tdr-code-reviewer** - Pure HTML/CSS/JS, cyberpunk theme, <50KB
- **tdr-design-system** - Black/green palette, Roboto font
- **tdr-feature-developer** - Landing page + X automation
- **tdr-ux-designer** - Minimalist constraints

### neuman-brain-* (Neuman Brain App)
- **neuman-brain-code-reviewer** - React/TypeScript, privacy-first
- **neuman-brain-design-system** - Dark mode, accessibility focus
- **neuman-brain-feature-developer** - Local-first architecture
- **neuman-brain-ux-designer** - Productivity app UX

### lazy-golfing-* (Lazy Golfing)
- **lazy-golfing-code-reviewer** - Next.js + NestJS, Prisma
- **lazy-golfing-design-system** - Miami Vice theme
- **lazy-golfing-feature-developer** - Full-stack patterns
- **lazy-golfing-ux-designer** - Golf course app UX

---

## How to Use Skills

### Automatic Activation
Skills activate when relevant context appears in conversation.

### Explicit Invocation
Request skills directly:
```
Use generic-code-reviewer to review this code
Use generic-design-system for the color choices
Use tech-debt-analyzer on this codebase
```

### Skill Tool
```
Skill(generic-code-reviewer)
Skill(tech-debt-analyzer)
```

---

## Skill Structure

Each skill follows this structure:

```
skills/
└── [skill-name]/
    ├── SKILL.md          # Main skill file (required)
    ├── references/       # Reference materials (optional)
    ├── assets/           # Templates, examples (optional)
    └── scripts/          # Helper scripts (optional)
```

### SKILL.md Format

```yaml
---
name: skill-name
description: Brief description for when Claude should use this skill
---

# Skill Title

Content with guidance, rules, patterns, examples...
```

---

## Creating New Skills

### 1. Create Folder
```bash
mkdir -p .claude/skills/[skill-name]
```

### 2. Create SKILL.md
- YAML frontmatter with name and description
- Comprehensive content for Claude to follow
- Examples and patterns

### 3. Add References (Optional)
Place in `references/` folder for additional context.

### 4. Test
Verify skill activates when expected context appears.

---

## Skill Categories

### By Tech Stack

| Stack | Recommended Skills |
|-------|-------------------|
| HTML/CSS/JS | generic-*, tdr-* |
| React/TypeScript | generic-*, neuman-brain-* |
| Next.js/NestJS | generic-*, lazy-golfing-* |
| Python | generic-code-reviewer, tech-debt-analyzer |
| Any | codebase-documenter, test-specialist |

### By Task Type

| Task | Recommended Skills |
|------|-------------------|
| Code review | generic-code-reviewer, [project]-code-reviewer |
| New feature | generic-feature-developer |
| UI/Styling | generic-design-system |
| UX design | generic-ux-designer |
| Documentation | codebase-documenter |
| Code health | tech-debt-analyzer |
| Testing | test-specialist |
| Documents | document-skills |

---

## Skills vs Other Claude Code Features

| Feature | Use For |
|---------|---------|
| **Skills** | Domain knowledge, guidelines, patterns |
| **Slash Commands** | Quick actions, workflows |
| **Hooks** | Automated responses to events |
| **Subagents** | Complex multi-step tasks |
| **MCP Servers** | External tool integration |

---

## Best Practices

### When to Create a Skill
- Repeated guidance needed
- Project-specific standards
- Complex domain knowledge
- Reusable patterns

### When NOT to Create a Skill
- One-time instructions
- Simple preferences (use CLAUDE.md)
- External tool integration (use MCP)

### Skill Content Tips
- Be comprehensive but focused
- Include concrete examples
- Reference other skills when relevant
- Keep updated as project evolves

---

## See Also

- `.claude/automation-guide.md` - Workflow automation
- `.claude/workflow-checklist.md` - Development checklist
- `.claude/commands/` - Custom slash commands
- `.claude/templates/` - Task templates
- `CLAUDE.md` - Project workflow rules

---

## Questions?

- Skills not activating? Check skill name and description match context
- Need new skill? Follow existing patterns in this folder
- Skill conflicts? Review descriptions for proper use cases

| **seo-analytics-auditor** | SEO analysis | Performance audits |

---

## Project-Specific Skills (Examples)

These skills show how to customize for specific projects:

### tdr-* (thedeadrobot.com)
- **tdr-code-reviewer** - Pure HTML/CSS/JS, cyberpunk theme, <50KB
- **tdr-design-system** - Black/green palette, Roboto font

## How to Use Skills

### Automatic Activation

### Explicit Invocation

Use tech-debt-analyzer on this codebase
```

```

---

## Skill Structure

```
skills/
└── [skill-name]/

    ├── references/       # Reference materials (optional)
    ├── assets/           # Templates, examples (optional)
    └── scripts/          # Helper scripts (optional)
```

### SKILL.md Format

name: skill-name
description: Brief description for when Claude should use this skill
---

Content with guidance, rules, patterns, examples...
```

---

## Creating New Skills

mkdir -p .claude/skills/[skill-name]

## Best Practices

### When NOT to Create a Skill

## 2.0.69

- Minor bugfixes

## 2.0.68

- Fixed IME (Input Method Editor) support for languages like Chinese, Japanese, and Korean by correctly positioning the composition window at the cursor
- Fixed a bug where disallowed MCP tools were visible to the model
- Fixed an issue where steering messages could be lost while a subagent is working
- Fixed Option+Arrow word navigation treating entire CJK (Chinese, Japanese, Korean) text sequences as a single word instead of navigating by word boundaries
- Improved plan mode exit UX: show simplified yes/no dialog when exiting with empty or missing plan instead of throwing an error
- Add support for enterprise managed settings. Contact your Anthropic account team to enable this feature.

## 2.0.67

- Thinking mode is now enabled by default for Opus 4.5
- Thinking mode configuration has moved to /config
- Added search functionality to `/permissions` command with `/` keyboard shortcut for filtering rules by tool name
- Show reason why autoupdater is disabled in `/doctor`
- Fixed false "Another process is currently updating Claude" error when running `claude update` while another instance is already on the latest version
- Fixed MCP servers from `.mcp.json` being stuck in pending state when running in non-interactive mode (`-p` flag or piped input)
- Fixed scroll position resetting after deleting a permission rule in `/permissions`
- Fixed word deletion (opt+delete) and word navigation (opt+arrow) not working correctly with non-Latin text such as Cyrillic, Greek, Arabic, Hebrew, Thai, and Chinese
- Fixed `claude install --force` not bypassing stale lock files
- Fixed consecutive @~/ file references in CLAUDE.md being incorrectly parsed due to markdown strikethrough interference
- Windows: Fixed plugin MCP servers failing due to colons in log directory paths

## 2.0.65

- Added ability to switch models while writing a prompt using alt+p (linux, windows), option+p (macos).
- Added context window information to status line input
- Added `fileSuggestion` setting for custom `@` file search commands
- Added `CLAUDE_CODE_SHELL` environment variable to override automatic shell detection (useful when login shell differs from actual working shell)
- Fixed prompt not being saved to history when aborting a query with Escape
- Fixed Read tool image handling to identify format from bytes instead of file extension

## 2.0.64

- Made auto-compacting instant
- Agents and bash commands can run asynchronously and send messages to wake up the main agent
- /stats now provides users with interesting CC stats, such as favorite model, usage graph, usage streak
- Added named session support: use `/rename` to name sessions, `/resume <name>` in REPL or `claude --resume <name>` from the terminal to resume them
- Added support for .claude/rules/`.  See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory for details.
